BucketGraph Class Reference

Detailed Description

Represents a graph structure used for bucket-based optimization in a solver.

The BucketGraph class provides various functionalities for managing and optimizing a graph structure using buckets. It includes methods for initialization, configuration printing, node visitation checks, statistics printing, bucket assignment, and more.

The class also supports parallel arc generation, label management, and feasibility checks. It is designed to work with different directions (Forward and Backward) and stages of the optimization process.

Note
This class relies on several preprocessor directives (e.g., RIH, RCC, SRC) to conditionally enable or disable certain features.
Template Parameters
DirectionThe direction of the graph (Forward or Backward).
StageThe stage of the optimization process.
FullIndicates whether the full labeling algorithm should be used.
ArcTypeThe type of arc (Bucket or regular).
MutabilityIndicates whether the label is mutable or immutable.

BucketGraph::BucketGraph ( const std::vector< VRPNode > & nodes,
int horizon,
int bucket_interval,
int capacity,
int capacity_interval )

Constructs a BucketGraph object.

This constructor initializes a BucketGraph with the given nodes, time horizon, and bucket interval. It sets up the forward and backward buckets, initializes the dual values for the CVRP separation, and defines the intervals and resource limits.

◆ assign_buckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D>
auto & BucketGraph::assign_buckets ( auto & FW,
auto & BW )
inlineconstexprnoexcept

Assigns the buckets based on the specified direction.

This function returns a reference to the buckets based on the specified direction. If the direction is Forward, it returns a reference to the forward buckets. If the direction is Backward, it returns a reference to the backward buckets.

Template Parameters
DThe direction (Forward or Backward).
Parameters
FWThe forward buckets.
BWThe backward buckets.
Returns
A reference to the buckets based on the specified direction.

◆ augment_ng_memories()

void BucketGraph::augment_ng_memories ( std::vector< double > & solution,
std::vector< Path > & paths,
bool aggressive,
int eta1,
int eta2,
int eta_max,
int nC )

Augments the memories in the BucketGraph.

This function takes a solution vector, a SparseModel, and several parameters to augment the memories in the BucketGraph. It identifies cycles in the SparseModel that meet certain conditions and forbids them in the BucketGraph. The function prioritizes smaller cycles and limits the number of forbidden cycles based on the given parameters.

Definition at line 349 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

351 {
352 // Pre-allocate with estimated size to avoid reallocations
353 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> cycles;
354 cycles.reserve(paths.size() /
355 4); // Estimate 25% of paths might have cycles
356
357 // Detect cycles in fractional paths
358 for (size_t col = 0; col < paths.size(); ++col) {
359 const double epsilon = 1e-2;
360 if (solution[col] <= epsilon || solution[col] >= 1 - epsilon) continue;
361
362 const auto &path = paths[col];
363 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<uint16_t, int> visited_clients;
364 visited_clients.reserve(path.size());
365
366 for (size_t i = 0; i < path.size(); ++i) {
367 const uint16_t client = static_cast<uint16_t>(path[i]);
368 if (client == 0 || client == N_SIZE - 1) continue;
369
370 auto it = visited_clients.find(client);
371 if (it != visited_clients.end()) {
372 // Cycle found - extract it
373 std::vector<uint16_t> cycle;
374 cycle.reserve(i - it->second + 1);
375
376 for (size_t j = it->second; j <= i; ++j) {
377 cycle.push_back(static_cast<uint16_t>(path[j]));
378 }
379
380 cycles.push_back(cycle);
381 break;
382 }
383 visited_clients[client] = i;
384 }
385 }
386
387 if (cycles.empty()) return;
388
389 // Sort cycles by size
390 pdqsort(cycles.begin(), cycles.end(),
391 [](const auto &a, const auto &b) { return a.size() < b.size(); });
392
393 // Process cycles
394 int forbidden_count = 0;
395
396 // Lambda for counting bits in neighborhoods
397 auto count_neighborhood_bits = [this, eta_max](uint16_t node) -> int {
398 int count = 0;
399 for (const auto &segment : neighborhoods_bitmap[node]) {
400 count += __builtin_popcountll(segment);
401 if (count >= eta_max) return count;
402 }
403 return count;
404 };
405
406 for (const auto &cycle : cycles) {
407 if (cycle.empty()) continue;
408
409 // Check if cycle can be forbidden
410 bool can_forbid = true;
411 for (const uint16_t node : cycle) {
412 if (count_neighborhood_bits(node) >= eta_max) {
413 can_forbid = false;
414 break;
415 }
416 }
417
418 if (can_forbid && (cycle.size() <= eta1 || forbidden_count < eta2)) {
419 // Convert uint16_t vector to int vector for forbidCycle
420 std::vector<int> int_cycle(cycle.begin(), cycle.end());
421 forbidCycle(int_cycle, aggressive);
422 if (++forbidden_count >= eta2) break;
423 }
424 }
425}

std::vector< std::vector< uint64_t > > neighborhoods_bitmap

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::bi_labeling_algorithm ( )

Performs the bi-labeling algorithm on the BucketGraph.

418 {
419 // Stage-specific initializations
420 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three) {
421 heuristic_fixing<S>();
422 } else if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
423 if (first_reset) {
424 reset_fixed();
425 first_reset = false;
426 }
427#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
428 if (options.bucket_fixing) {
429 bucket_fixing<S>();
430 }
431#endif
432 }
433
434 reset_pool();
435 common_initialization();
436
437 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size());
438 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size());
439
440 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
441 run_labeling_algorithms<S, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
442 } else {
443 forward_cbar =
444 labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, S, Full::Partial>();
445 }
446
447 auto best_label = label_pool_fw->acquire();
448 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
449 if (check_feasibility(fw_best_label, bw_best_label)) {
450 best_label = compute_label<S>(fw_best_label, bw_best_label);
451 } else {
452 best_label->cost = 0.0;
453 best_label->real_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
454 best_label->nodes_covered.clear();
455 }
456 } else {
457 best_label->cost = best_label->real_cost;
458 best_label->real_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
459 best_label->nodes_covered.clear();
460 }
461 merged_labels.push_back(best_label);
462
463 if constexpr (S == Stage::Enumerate) {
464 fmt::print("Labels generated, concatenating...\n");
465 }
466
467 const size_t n_segments = (fw_buckets_size + 63) / 64;
468 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments);
469
470 double best_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
471 for (int bucket = 0; bucket < fw_buckets_size; ++bucket) {
472 const auto &bucket_labels = fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
473
474 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
475 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket += bucket_labels.size();
476 }
477
478 for (const Label *L : bucket_labels) {
479 const auto &to_arcs =
480 nodes[L->node_id].get_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
481
482 for (const auto &arc : to_arcs) {
483 const int to_node = arc.to;
484
485 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Eliminate) {
486 if (fixed_arcs[L->node_id][to_node] == 1) {
487 continue;
488 }
489 }
490
491 auto extended_labels =
492 Extend<Direction::Forward, S, ArcType::Node,
493 Mutability::Const, Full::Reverse>(L, arc);
494 for (Label *L_prime : extended_labels) {
495 int bucket_to_process =
496 L_prime->vertex; // Create mutable copy
497 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0,
498 n_segments * sizeof(uint64_t));
499 ConcatenateLabel<S, SYM>(L, bucket_to_process, best_cost,
500 Bvisited);
501 }
502 }
503 }
504 }
505
506 pdqsort(merged_labels.begin(), merged_labels.end(),
507 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
508
509#ifdef SCHRODINGER
510 if (merged_labels.size() > N_ADD) {
511 std::vector<Path> paths;
512 const int labels_size = merged_labels.size();
513 const int end_idx = std::min(N_ADD + N_ADD, labels_size);
514
515 paths.reserve(end_idx - N_ADD);
516 for (int i = N_ADD; i < end_idx; ++i) {
517 if (merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) {
518 continue;
519 }
520 paths.emplace_back(merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered,
521 merged_labels[i]->real_cost);
522 }
523
524 sPool.add_paths(paths);
525 sPool.iterate();
526 }
527#endif
528
529#ifdef RIH
530
531 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
532 std::vector<Label *> top_labels;
533 top_labels.reserve(N_ADD);
534
535 const int n_candidates =
536 std::min(N_ADD, static_cast<int>(merged_labels.size()));
537 for (int i = 0; i < n_candidates; ++i) {
538 if (merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) {
539 continue;
540 }
541 top_labels.push_back(merged_labels[i]);
542 }
543 ils->submit_task(top_labels, nodes);
544 }
545#endif
546
547 inner_obj = merged_labels[0]->cost;
548 return merged_labels;
549}

◆ bucket_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::bucket_fixing ( )

Fixes the bucket arcs for the specified stage.

This function performs the bucket arc fixing for the given stage. It initializes necessary variables and runs labeling algorithms to compute forward and backward reduced costs. Based on the computed gap, it performs arc elimination in both forward and backward directions and generates the necessary arcs.

Definition at line 966 of file BucketUtils.h.

966 {
967 // Stage 4 bucket arc fixing
968 if (!fixed) {
969 fmt::print("\033[34m_STARTING BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE \033[0m");
970 fmt::print("\n");
971 fixed = true;
972 common_initialization();
973
974 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(
975 fw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
976 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(
977 bw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
978
979 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Four, Full::Full>(forward_cbar,
980 backward_cbar);
981
982 gap = std::ceil(incumbent - (relaxation + std::min(0.0, min_red_cost)));
983
984 // check if gap is -inf and early exit, due to IPM
985 if (gap < 0) {
986 fmt::print(
987 "\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE CAN'T BE EXECUTED DUE TO "
988 "GAP\033[0m");
989 fmt::print("\n");
990 // print the gap
991 fmt::print("gap: {}\n", gap);
992 return;
993 }
994 fw_c_bar = forward_cbar;
995 bw_c_bar = backward_cbar;
996
997 print_info("performing bucket arc elimination with theta = {}\n", gap);
998
999 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1000 work, bi_sched,
1001 SECTION {
1002 // Section 1: Forward direction
1003 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Forward>(gap);
1004 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Forward>();
1005 },
1006 SECTION {
1007 // Section 2: Backward direction
1008 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Backward>(gap);
1009 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Backward>();
1010 });
1011
1012 generate_arcs();
1013 fmt::print("\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE FINISHED\033[0m");
1014 fmt::print("\n");
1015 just_fixed = true;
1016 return;
1017 }
1018 just_fixed = false;
1019}

◆ BucketArcElimination()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::BucketArcElimination ( double theta)

Eliminates bucket arcs based on a given threshold.

This function performs bucket arc elimination in either the forward or backward direction, depending on the template parameter D. It resets all elements in the fixed buckets to 0, prints information about the direction of elimination, and processes arcs to update the local bucket arc map and fixed buckets.

176 {
177 // Assign forward or backward buckets, adjacency lists, and fixed buckets
178 // based on direction
179 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
180 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
181 auto &Phi_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_bw, Phi_fw);
182 auto &fixed_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets, bw_fixed_buckets);
183 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
184 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap =
185 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
186 // Reset fixed_buckets
187 auto n_buckets = buckets_size;
188
189 // for (auto &fb : fixed_buckets) { std::fill(fb.begin(), fb.end(), 0); }
190 // set fixed_buckets_bitmap all to 0
191 fixed_buckets_bitmap.assign(fixed_buckets_bitmap.size(), 0);
192
193 using ArcMap =
194 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, int>,
195 ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int>,
196 arc_map_hash>;
197
198 ArcMap local_B_Ba_b;
199 int removed_arcs = 0;
200
201 auto create_arc_key = [](int from, int to, int b) {
202 return std::make_pair(std::make_pair(from, to), b);
203 };
204
205 // Function to process jump arcs
206 auto process_jump_arcs = [&](int b) {
207 const auto &jump_arcs = buckets[b].template get_jump_arcs<D>();
208 if (!jump_arcs.empty()) {
209 const size_t n_segments = buckets_size / 64 + 1;
210 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
211 auto labels = buckets[b].get_labels();
212 for (const auto &a : jump_arcs) {
213 auto increment = a.resource_increment;
214
215 // auto arc_key =
216 // std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.base_bucket, a.jump_bucket),
217 // b);
218 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(a.base_bucket, a.jump_bucket, b);
219 int b_opposite =
220 get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.jump_bucket, increment);
221
222 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
223
224 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
225 Bidi_map.insert(b);
226 if (!Bvisited.empty()) {
227 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0,
228 Bvisited.size() * sizeof(uint64_t));
229 }
230 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite,
231 Bvisited);
232 }
233 }
234 }
235 };
236
237 // Function to process bucket arcs
238 auto process_bucket_arcs = [&](int b) {
239 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
240 auto labels = buckets[b].get_labels();
241
242 const size_t n_segments = buckets_size / 64 + 1;
243 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
244
245 for (const auto &a : bucket_arcs) {
246 std::vector<double> increment(options.resources.size(), 0);
247 // print increment.size
248 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
249 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
250 increment[r] = buckets[b].lb[r] + a.resource_increment[r];
251 } else {
252 increment[r] = buckets[b].ub[r] - a.resource_increment[r];
253 }
254 }
255 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(buckets[a.from_bucket].node_id,
256 buckets[a.to_bucket].node_id, b);
257 int b_opposite =
258 get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.to_bucket, increment);
259 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
260
261 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
262 Bidi_map.insert(b);
263 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0,
264 Bvisited.size() * sizeof(uint64_t));
265 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite,
266 Bvisited);
267 }
268
269 // Check for opposite direction arcs
270 if (auto it = local_B_Ba_b.find(arc_key);
271 it != local_B_Ba_b.end()) {
272 auto &Bidi_map_opposite = it->second;
273 bool contains = std::any_of(
274 Phi_opposite[b_opposite].begin(),
275 Phi_opposite[b_opposite].end(), [&](int b_prime) {
276 return Bidi_map_opposite.find(b_prime) !=
277 Bidi_map_opposite.end();
278 });
279
280 if (!contains && Bidi_map_opposite.find(b_opposite) ==
281 Bidi_map_opposite.end()) {
282 size_t bit_pos = a.from_bucket * n_buckets + a.to_bucket;
283 fixed_buckets_bitmap[bit_pos / 64] |=
284 (1ULL << (bit_pos % 64));
285
286 ++removed_arcs;
287 }
288 }
289 }
290 };
291
292 // Process each bucket
293 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
294 const auto &node_arcs =
295 nodes[buckets[b].node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
296
297 // Process node arcs
298 for (const auto &a : node_arcs) {
299 auto arc_key = std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b);
300 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
301
302 if (!Phi[b].empty()) {
303 Bidi_map.insert(b);
304 for (const auto &b_prime : Phi[b]) {
305 auto neighbor_key =
306 std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b_prime);
307 if (auto it = local_B_Ba_b.find(neighbor_key);
308 it != local_B_Ba_b.end()) {
309 Bidi_map.insert(it->second.begin(), it->second.end());
310 }
311 }
312 }
313 }
314
315 // Process jump and bucket arcs using separate functions
316 process_jump_arcs(b);
317 process_bucket_arcs(b);
318 }
319
320 // Print the number of arcs removed
321 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
322 print_info("[Fw] removed arcs: {}\n", removed_arcs);
323 } else {
324 print_info("[Bw] removed arcs: {}\n", removed_arcs);
325 }
326}

◆ calculate_neighborhoods()

void BucketGraph::calculate_neighborhoods ( size_t num_closest)

Calculates the neighborhoods for each node for the ng-routes.

Definition at line 289 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

289 {
290 size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
291
292 // Initialize the neighborhood bitmaps as vectors of uint64_t for forward
293 // and backward neighborhoods
294 neighborhoods_bitmap.resize(num_nodes); // Forward neighborhood
295
296 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
297 std::vector<std::pair<double, size_t>>
298 forward_distances; // Distances for forward neighbors
299
300 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
301 if (i != j) {
302 // Forward distance (i -> j)
303 double forward_distance = getcij(i, j);
304 forward_distances.push_back({forward_distance, j});
305 }
306 }
307
308 // Sort distances to find the closest nodes
309 pdqsort(forward_distances.begin(), forward_distances.end());
310
311 // Initialize the neighborhood bitmap vector for node i (forward and
312 // backward)
313 size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
314 neighborhoods_bitmap[i].resize(num_segments,
315 0); // Resizing for forward bitmap
316
317 // Include the node itself in both forward and backward neighborhoods
318 size_t segment_self = i >> 6;
319 size_t bit_position_self = i & 63;
320 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][segment_self] |=
321 (1ULL << bit_position_self); // Forward
322
323 // Map the top 'num_closest' closest nodes for forward and set them in
324 // the backward neighborhoods
325 for (size_t k = 0; k < num_closest && k < forward_distances.size();
326 ++k) {
327 size_t node_index = forward_distances[k].second;
328
329 // Determine the segment and the bit within the segment for the
330 // node_index (forward)
331 size_t segment = node_index >> 6;
332 size_t bit_position = node_index & 63;
333 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][segment] |=
334 (1ULL << bit_position); // Forward neighbor
335 }
336 }
337}

◆ check_feasibility()

bool BucketGraph::check_feasibility ( const Label * fw_label,
const Label * bw_label )
inline

Checks the feasibility of a given forward and backward label.

This function determines if the transition from a forward label to a backward label is feasible based on resource constraints and node durations.

Definition at line 781 of file BucketGraph.h.

782 {
783 if (!fw_label || !bw_label) return false;
784
785 // Cache resources and node data
786 const auto &fw_resources = fw_label->resources;
787 const auto &bw_resources = bw_label->resources;
788 const struct VRPNode &VRPNode = nodes[fw_label->node_id];
789
790 // Time feasibility check
791 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
792 if (options.resources[r] != "time") {
793 continue;
794 }
795 const auto time_fw = fw_resources[r];
796 const auto time_bw = bw_resources[r];
797 const auto travel_time =
798 getcij(fw_label->node_id, bw_label->node_id);
799 if (time_fw + travel_time + VRPNode.duration > time_bw) {
800 return false;
801 }
802 }
803
804 // Resource feasibility check (if applicable)
805 if (options.resources.size() > 1) {
806 for (size_t i = 1; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
807 const auto resource_fw = fw_resources[i];
808 const auto resource_bw = bw_resources[i];
809 const auto demand = VRPNode.demand;
810
811 if (resource_fw + demand > resource_bw) {
812 return false;
813 }
814 }
815 }
816
817 return true;
818 }

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::common_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph by clearing previous data and setting up forward and backward buckets.

1285 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
1286 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
1287 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
1288
1289 auto &warm_labels = assign_buckets<D>(fw_warm_labels, bw_warm_labels);
1290 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
1291 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
1292 auto &c_bar = assign_buckets<D>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
1293 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
1294
1295 if (merged_labels.size() > 0 && options.warm_start && !just_fixed) {
1296 warm_labels.reserve(buckets_size);
1297 warm_labels.clear();
1298 warm_labels.reserve(buckets_size);
1299 for (auto bucket = 0; bucket < buckets_size; ++bucket) {
1300 if (auto *label = buckets[bucket].get_best_label()) {
1301 warm_labels.push_back(label);
1302 }
1303 }
1304 }
1305
1306 // Initialize vectors with exact sizes once
1307 c_bar.resize(buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1308 // print size of c_bar
1309
1310 if constexpr (Direction::Forward == D) {
1311 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(buckets_size + 1, 0);
1312 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
1313 }
1314
1315 const auto &VRPNode = nodes[0];
1316
1317 for (size_t b = 0; b < buckets_size; b++) {
1318 buckets[b].clear();
1319 }
1320
1321 // Calculate intervals once
1322 for (size_t r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
1323 base_intervals[r] =
1324 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / bucket_splits[VRPNode.id];
1325 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
1326 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
1327 }
1328
1329 if (options.warm_start && !just_fixed) {
1330 pdqsort(
1331 warm_labels.begin(), warm_labels.end(),
1332 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
1333
1334 std::vector<Label *> processed_labels;
1335 const size_t process_size = std::min(
1336 warm_labels.size(), static_cast<size_t>(options.n_warm_start));
1337 processed_labels.reserve(process_size);
1338
1339 for (size_t i = 0; i < process_size; ++i) {
1340 auto label = warm_labels[i];
1341 if (!label->fresh) continue;
1342
1343 auto new_label = compute_red_cost(label, true);
1344 if (new_label != nullptr) {
1345 fw_buckets[new_label->vertex].add_label(new_label);
1346 processed_labels.push_back(new_label);
1347 }
1348 }
1349 warm_labels = std::move(processed_labels);
1350 }
1351
1352 // Initialize intervals
1353 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
1354 int offset = 0;
1355
1356 // Lambda for interval combination generation
1357 auto generate_combinations = [&](bool is_forward, int &offset) {
1358 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
1359 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
1360 const auto depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
1361 const int calculated_index_base =
1362 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot]
1363 : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
1364
1365 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
1366 std::function<void(int)> process_intervals = [&](int depth) {
1367 if (depth == num_intervals) {
1368 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
1369 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
1370
1371 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1372 interval_bounds[r] =
1373 is_forward
1374 ? interval_starts[r] +
1375 current_pos[r] *
1376 roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r])
1377 : interval_ends[r] -
1378 current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(
1379 base_intervals[r]);
1380 }
1381
1382 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds,
1383 depot_id);
1384 depot->is_extended = false;
1385 depot->nodes_covered.push_back(depot_id);
1386 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
1387 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(
1388 depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->SRCDuals.size(), 0);)
1389 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
1390 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
1391
1392 offset++;
1393 return;
1394 }
1395
1396 for (int k = 0; k < bucket_splits[depot_id]; ++k) {
1397 current_pos[depth] = k;
1398 process_intervals(depth + 1);
1399 }
1400 };
1401
1402 process_intervals(0);
1403 };
1404
1405 // Process forward and backward directions
1406 if constexpr (Direction::Forward == D) {
1407 generate_combinations(true, offset);
1408 } else {
1409 generate_combinations(false, offset);
1410 }
1411}

◆ compute_mono_label()

Label * BucketGraph::compute_mono_label ( const Label * L)

Computes the mono label for the BucketGraph.

This function computes the mono label for the BucketGraph by acquiring a new label from the label pool and setting the cost and real cost values from the given label L. It then calculates the number of nodes covered by the label and its ancestors, reserves space for the nodes covered, and inserts the nodes from the

Label * BucketGraph::compute_red_cost ( Label * L,
bool fw )
inline

1070 {
1071 double real_cost = 0.0;
1072 double red_cost = 0.0;
1073
1074 const double main_resource = L->resources[options.main_resources[0]];
1075 const double q_star_value = q_star[options.main_resources[0]];
1076
1077 if (fw) {
1078 if (main_resource > q_star_value) {
1079 return nullptr;
1080 }
1081 } else {
1082 if (main_resource <= q_star_value) {
1083 return nullptr;
1084 }
1085 }
1086
1087 // Initialize an empty SRCmap for the current label
1088 std::vector<uint16_t> updated_SRCmap(cut_storage->size(), 0.0);
1089
1090 int last_node = -1;
1091 if (L->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) {
1092 return nullptr;
1093 }
1094 auto new_label = new Label();
1095
1096 // Traverse through the label nodes from current to root
1097 for (auto node_id : L->nodes_covered) {
1099 // NG-route feasibility check
1102 if (is_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id)) {
1103 return nullptr;
1104 }
1105
1106 size_t limit = new_label->visited_bitmap.size();
1107 for (size_t i = 0; i < limit; ++i) {
1108 uint64_t current_visited =
1109 new_label->visited_bitmap[i]; // Get visited nodes in the
1110 // current segment
1111
1112 if (!current_visited)
1113 continue; // Skip if no nodes were visited in this segment
1114
1115 uint64_t neighborhood_mask =
1116 neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][i]; // Get neighborhood mask
1117 // for the current node
1118 uint64_t bits_to_clear =
1119 current_visited &
1120 neighborhood_mask; // Determine which bits to clear
1121
1122 new_label->visited_bitmap[i] =
1123 bits_to_clear; // Clear irrelevant visited nodes
1124 }
1125 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap,
1126 node_id); // Mark the new node as visited
1127
1128 if (last_node != -1) {
1129 double cij_cost = getcij(last_node, node_id);
1130 real_cost += cij_cost;
1131 red_cost += cij_cost;
1132 }
1133
1134 red_cost -= nodes[node_id].cost;
1135
1137 // SRC logic
1140 size_t segment =
1141 node_id >> 6; // Determine the segment in the bitmap
1142 size_t bit_position =
1143 node_id & 63; // Determine the bit position in the segment
1144
1145 // Update the SRCmap for each cut
1146 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
1147 auto &SRCDuals = cutter->SRCDuals;
1148 const uint64_t bit_mask = 1ULL << bit_position;
1149
1150 for (std::size_t idx = 0; idx < cutter->size(); ++idx) {
1151 const auto &cut = cutter->getCut(idx);
1152 const auto &baseSet = cut.baseSet;
1153 const auto &baseSetorder = cut.baseSetOrder;
1154 const auto &neighbors = cut.neighbors;
1155 const auto &multipliers = cut.p;
1156
1157#if defined(SRC)
1158 // Apply SRC logic to update the SRCmap
1159 const bool bitIsSet = neighbors[segment] & bit_mask;
1160
1161 auto &src_map_value = updated_SRCmap[idx];
1162 if (!bitIsSet) {
1163 src_map_value =
1164 0.0; // Reset if bit is not set in neighbors
1165 continue;
1166 }
1167 const bool bitIsSet2 = baseSet[segment] & bit_mask;
1168
1169 if (bitIsSet2) {
1170 auto &den = multipliers.den;
1171 src_map_value += multipliers.num[baseSetorder[node_id]];
1172 if (src_map_value >= den) {
1173 red_cost -= SRCDuals[idx]; // Apply the SRC dual value
1174 // if threshold is exceeded
1175 src_map_value -= den; // Reset the SRC map value
1176 }
1177 }
1178#endif
1179 }
1180
1181 // Adjust for arc duals if in Stage::Four
1182#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
1183 if (last_node) {
1184 auto arc_dual = arc_duals.getDual(last_node, node_id);
1185 red_cost -= arc_dual;
1186 }
1187#endif
1188
1189 // Adjust for branching duals if they exist
1190 if (branching_duals->size() > 0 && last_node) {
1191 red_cost -= branching_duals->getDual(last_node, node_id);
1192 }
1193
1194 // Move to the parent node and update last_node
1195 last_node = node_id;
1196 }
1197
1198 new_label->cost = red_cost;
1199 new_label->real_cost = real_cost;
1200 new_label->parent = nullptr;
1201 new_label->node_id = L->node_id;
1202 new_label->nodes_covered = L->nodes_covered;
1203 new_label->is_extended = false;
1204 new_label->resources = L->resources;
1205 new_label->SRCmap = updated_SRCmap;
1206
1207 // Bucket number for the new label
1208 std::vector<double> new_resources(options.resources.size());
1209 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
1210 new_resources[i] = new_label->resources[i];
1211 }
1212 int bucket = fw ? get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(
1213 new_label->node_id, new_resources)
1214 : get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(
1215 new_label->node_id, new_resources);
1216 new_label->vertex = bucket;
1217 new_label->fresh = false;
1218
1219 return new_label;
1220 };

std::vector< int > BucketGraph::computePhi ( int & bucket_id,
bool fw )

Computes the phi values for a given bucket ID and direction.

Definition at line 196 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

196 {
197 std::vector<int> phi;
198
199 // Ensure bucket_id is within valid bounds
200 auto &buckets = fw ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
201 auto &fixed_buckets = fw ? fw_fixed_buckets : bw_fixed_buckets;
202 auto &node_interval_trees =
203 fw ? fw_node_interval_trees : bw_node_interval_trees;
204 auto &bucket_splits = fw ? fw_bucket_splits : bw_bucket_splits;
205 if (options.resources.size() > 1) {
206 if (bucket_id >= buckets.size() || bucket_id < 0) return phi;
207
208 std::vector<double> total_ranges(intervals.size());
209 std::vector<double> base_intervals(intervals.size());
210
211 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
212 total_ranges[r] =
213 R_max[r] - R_min[r]; // Ensure integer type for total range
214 base_intervals[r] = total_ranges[r] / bucket_splits[bucket_id];
215 }
216
217 // Get the node ID and current bucket
218 int node_id = buckets[bucket_id].node_id;
219 auto &current_bucket = buckets[bucket_id];
220
221 // Retrieve the pre-built Splay Tree for this node
222 auto &node_tree = node_interval_trees[node_id];
223
224 // Search for matching intervals using the existing Splay Tree
225 if (fw) {
226 // Forward search: find the interval just below the current bucket
227 std::vector<double> target_low = current_bucket.lb;
228 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
229 target_low[r] -=
230 base_intervals[r]; // Adjust for the base intervals
231 }
232
233 TreeNode *found_node = node_tree.find(target_low);
234 if (found_node != nullptr &&
235 buckets[found_node->bucket_index].node_id == node_id) {
236 // Check if the found bucket is fixed
237#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
238 if (is_bucket_not_fixed<Direction::Forward>(
239 found_node->bucket_index, bucket_id))
240#endif
241 {
242 phi.push_back(found_node->bucket_index);
243 }
244 }
245 } else {
246 // Backward search: find the interval just above the current bucket
247 std::vector<double> target_high = current_bucket.ub;
248 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
249 target_high[r] +=
250 base_intervals[r]; // Adjust for the base intervals
251 }
252
253 TreeNode *found_node = node_tree.find(target_high);
254 if (found_node != nullptr &&
255 buckets[found_node->bucket_index].node_id == node_id) {
256 // Check if the found bucket is fixed
257#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
258 if (is_bucket_not_fixed<Direction::Forward>(
259 found_node->bucket_index, bucket_id))
260#endif
261 {
262 phi.push_back(found_node->bucket_index);
263 }
264 }
265 }
266
267 } else {
268 // Handle the case where R_SIZE == 1 with a simpler approach
269 int smaller = bucket_id - 1;
270
271 if (smaller >= 0 &&
272 buckets[smaller].node_id == buckets[bucket_id].node_id) {
273#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
274 if (fw ? is_bucket_not_fixed_forward(smaller, bucket_id)
275 : is_bucket_not_fixed_backward(smaller, bucket_id))
276#endif
277 {
278 phi.push_back(smaller);
279 }
280 }
281 }
282 return phi;
283}

580 // Reuse thread-local bucket_stack to avoid repeated allocations
581 static thread_local std::vector<int> bucket_stack;
582 bucket_stack.clear();
583 bucket_stack.reserve(50); // Adjust based on expected size
584 bucket_stack.push_back(b);
585
586 auto &other_buckets = assign_symmetry<SYM>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
587 auto &other_c_bar = assign_symmetry<SYM>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
588
589 // Cache frequently accessed values
590 const int L_node_id = L->node_id;
591 const auto &L_resources = L->resources;
592 const auto &L_last_node = nodes[L_node_id];
593 const double L_cost = L->cost;
594 const size_t bitmap_size = L->visited_bitmap.size();
595
596 // Pre-compute constants for bit operations
597 constexpr uint64_t one = 1ULL;
598 const bool has_branching = !branching_duals->empty();
599
600 // SRC mode setup
601#if defined(SRC)
602 decltype(cut_storage) cutter = nullptr;
603 if constexpr (S > Stage::Three) {
604 cutter = cut_storage;
605 }
606 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
607#endif
608
609 while (!bucket_stack.empty()) {
610 const int current_bucket = bucket_stack.back();
611 bucket_stack.pop_back();
612
613 // Optimize bit operations for visited tracking
614 const size_t segment = current_bucket >> 6;
615 const uint64_t bit_mask = one << (current_bucket & 63);
616 Bvisited[segment] |= bit_mask;
617
618 const int bucketLprimenode = other_buckets[current_bucket].node_id;
619 double travel_cost = getcij(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
620
621 // Apply arc duals if needed
622#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
623 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
624 travel_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
625 }
626#endif
627
628 if (has_branching) {
629 travel_cost -=
630 branching_duals->getDual(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
631 }
632
633 const double path_cost = L_cost + travel_cost;
634 const double bound = other_c_bar[current_bucket];
635
636 // Early bound check
637 if ((S != Stage::Enumerate && path_cost + bound >= best_cost) ||
638 (S == Stage::Enumerate && path_cost + bound >= gap)) {
639 continue;
640 }
641
642 // Process labels in current bucket
643 const auto &bucket = other_buckets[current_bucket];
644 const auto &labels = bucket.get_labels();
645
646// Parallelize the inner loop if possible
647#pragma omp parallel for schedule(dynamic) reduction(min : best_cost)
648 for (size_t i = 0; i < labels.size(); ++i) {
649 const Label *L_bw = labels[i];
650
651 // Early rejection tests
652 if (L_bw->node_id == L_node_id || !check_feasibility(L, L_bw)) {
653 continue;
654 }
655
656 // Visited nodes overlap check
657 if constexpr (S >= Stage::Three) {
658 bool has_overlap = false;
659 for (size_t j = 0; j < bitmap_size; ++j) {
660 if (L->visited_bitmap[j] & L_bw->visited_bitmap[j]) {
661 has_overlap = true;
662 break;
663 }
664 }
665 if (has_overlap) continue;
666 }
667
668 double total_cost = path_cost + L_bw->cost;
669
670 // SRC cost adjustments
671#if defined(SRC)
672 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
673 for (const auto &active_cut : active_cuts) {
674 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
675 const size_t idx = active_cut.index;
676 const double dual = active_cut.dual_value;
677
678 if (L->SRCmap[idx] + L_bw->SRCmap[idx] >= cut.p.den) {
679 total_cost -= dual;
680 }
681 }
682 }
683#endif
684
685 // Cost-based filtering
686 if ((S != Stage::Enumerate && total_cost >= best_cost) ||
687 (S == Stage::Enumerate && total_cost >= gap)) {
688 continue;
689 }
690
691 // Create new merged label
692 auto pbest = compute_label<S>(L, L_bw);
693#pragma omp critical
694 {
695 if (pbest->cost < best_cost) {
696 best_cost = pbest->cost;
697 }
698 merged_labels.push_back(pbest);
699 }
700 }
701
702 // Process neighbor buckets
703 for (int b_prime : Phi_bw[current_bucket]) {
704 const size_t seg_prime = b_prime >> 6;
705 const uint64_t mask_prime = one << (b_prime & 63);
706 if (!(Bvisited[seg_prime] & mask_prime)) {
707 bucket_stack.push_back(b_prime);
708 }
709 }
710 }
711}

◆ define_buckets()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::define_buckets ( )

Defines the buckets for the BucketGraph.

This function determines the number of buckets based on the time intervals and assigns buckets to the graph. It computes resource bounds for each vertex and defines the bounds of each bucket.

119 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
120 auto old_size = buckets.size();
121
122 // Store the range of fixed buckets for each node pair
123 std::unordered_map<
124 int, std::unordered_map<
125 int, std::pair<std::vector<double>, std::vector<double>>>>
126 node_pair_fixed_ranges;
127
128 if (old_size > 0) {
129 old_buckets = buckets;
130 was_fixed.resize(old_size * old_size, false);
131 auto &old_bitmap =
132 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);

134 // First pass: mark which bucket pairs were fixed
135 for (size_t i = 0; i < old_size; ++i) {
136 for (size_t j = 0; j < old_size; ++j) {
137 size_t old_bit_pos = i * old_size + j;
138 if (old_bit_pos / 64 < old_bitmap.size()) {
139 was_fixed[old_bit_pos] =
140 (old_bitmap[old_bit_pos / 64] &
141 (1ULL << (old_bit_pos % 64))) != 0;

143 }
144 }
145
146 const int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
147 // For each fixed bucket pair, track the range by node pair
148 for (size_t i = 0; i < old_size; ++i) {
149 for (size_t j = 0; j < old_size; ++j) {
150 if (!was_fixed[i * old_size + j]) continue;
151
152 const auto &bucket_from = old_buckets[i];
153 const auto &bucket_to = old_buckets[j];
154
155 int from_node = bucket_from.node_id;
156 int to_node = bucket_to.node_id;
157
158 // Initialize ranges for this node pair if not already done
159 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].find(to_node) ==
160 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].end()) {
161 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node] = {
162 std::vector<double>(num_intervals,
163 std::numeric_limits<double>::max()),
164 std::vector<double>(
165 num_intervals,
166 std::numeric_limits<double>::lowest())};
167 }
168
169 // Update the ranges for this node pair
170 auto &[min_lbs, max_ubs] =
171 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node];
172 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
173 min_lbs[r] = std::min(min_lbs[r], bucket_from.lb[r]);
174 max_ubs[r] = std::max(max_ubs[r], bucket_from.ub[r]);
175 }
176 }
177 }
178

180 // for (const auto &[from_node, to_nodes] : node_pair_fixed_ranges) {

191 }
192
193 // Clear existing buckets and bitmap
194 buckets.clear();
195 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap =
196 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
197 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
198
199 const int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
200 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
201 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
202
203 // Get references to direction-specific containers
204 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
205 auto &num_buckets_index =
206 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
207 auto &node_interval_trees =
208 assign_buckets<D>(fw_node_interval_trees, bw_node_interval_trees);
209 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
210 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
211
212 // Pre-allocate containers
213 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
214 num_buckets.resize(num_nodes);
215 num_buckets_index.resize(num_nodes);
216 node_interval_trees.assign(num_nodes, SplayTree());
217
218 // Lambda for interval calculations
219 auto calculate_interval = [](double lb, double ub, double base_interval,
220 int pos, int max_interval,
221 bool is_forward) -> std::pair<double, double> {
222 double start, end;
223 if (is_forward) {
224 start = lb + pos * base_interval;
225 end =
226 (pos == max_interval - 1) ? ub : lb + (pos + 1) * base_interval;
227 } else {
228 start =
229 (pos == max_interval - 1) ? lb : ub - (pos + 1) * base_interval;
230 end = ub - pos * base_interval;
231 }
232 return {roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(start), roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(end)};
233 };
234
235 int cum_sum = 0;
236 int bucket_index = 0;
237
238 // Temporary vectors for interval calculations
239 std::vector<double> interval_start(num_intervals);
240 std::vector<double> interval_end(num_intervals);
241 std::vector<int> pos(num_intervals, 0);
242
243 // Process each node
244 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
245 std::vector<double> node_base_interval(num_intervals);
246
247 // check if fw_bucket_splits with node_id exists
248 if (bucket_splits.find(VRPNode.id) == bucket_splits.end()) {
249 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
250 node_base_interval[r] =
251 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
252 }
253 bucket_splits[VRPNode.id] = intervals[0].interval;
254 } else {
255 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
256 node_base_interval[r] =
257 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / bucket_splits[VRPNode.id];
258 }
259 }
260
261 SplayTree node_tree;
262 int n_buckets = 0;
263
264 if (num_intervals == 1) {
265 // Single interval case
266 for (int j = 0; j < bucket_splits[VRPNode.id]; ++j) {
267 auto [start, end] = calculate_interval(
268 VRPNode.lb[0], VRPNode.ub[0], node_base_interval[0], j,
269 bucket_splits[VRPNode.id], D == Direction::Forward);
270
271 interval_start[0] = start;
272 interval_end[0] = end;
273
274 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
275 interval_start[0] =
276 std::max(interval_start[0], VRPNode.lb[0]);
277 } else {
278 interval_end[0] = std::min(interval_end[0], VRPNode.ub[0]);
279 }
280
281 buckets.push_back(
282 Bucket(VRPNode.id, interval_start, interval_end));
283 node_tree.insert(interval_start, interval_end, bucket_index++);
284 n_buckets++;
285 cum_sum++;
286 }
287 } else {
288 fmt::print("Multiple intervals\n");
289 // Multiple intervals case
290 std::fill(pos.begin(), pos.end(), 0);
291 do {
292 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
293 auto [start, end] = calculate_interval(
294 VRPNode.lb[r], VRPNode.ub[r], node_base_interval[r],
295 pos[r], intervals[r].interval, D == Direction::Forward);
296
297 interval_start[r] = start;
298 interval_end[r] = end;
299
300 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
301 interval_start[r] =
302 std::max(interval_start[r], R_min[r]);
303 } else {
304 interval_end[r] = std::min(interval_end[r], R_max[r]);
305 }
306 }
307
308 buckets.push_back(
309 Bucket(VRPNode.id, interval_start, interval_end));
310 node_tree.insert(interval_start, interval_end, bucket_index++);
311 n_buckets++;
312 cum_sum++;
313
314 // Generate next combination
315 int i = 0;
316 while (i < num_intervals && ++pos[i] >= intervals[i].interval) {
317 pos[i] = 0;
318 i++;
319 }
320 if (i == num_intervals) break;
321 } while (true);
322 }
323
324 // Update node-specific data
325 num_buckets[VRPNode.id] = n_buckets;
326 num_buckets_index[VRPNode.id] = cum_sum - n_buckets;
327 node_interval_trees[VRPNode.id] = node_tree;
328 }
329
330 if (!node_pair_fixed_ranges.empty()) {
331 size_t new_size = buckets.size();
332 size_t required_bitmap_words =
333 std::max(size_t(1), ((new_size * new_size) + 63) / 64);
334
335 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
336 fixed_buckets_bitmap.resize(required_bitmap_words, 0);
337
338 for (size_t from = 0; from < new_size; ++from) {
339 const auto &bucket_from = buckets[from];
340 int from_node = bucket_from.node_id;
341
342 // Skip if no outgoing fixed arcs from this node
343 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges.find(from_node) ==
344 node_pair_fixed_ranges.end())
345 continue;
346
347 for (size_t to = 0; to < new_size; ++to) {
348 const auto &bucket_to = buckets[to];
349 int to_node = bucket_to.node_id;
350
351 // Skip if this arc wasn't fixed before
352 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].find(to_node) ==
353 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].end())
354 continue;
355
356 const auto &range = node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node];
357
358 // Check if both buckets are within their fixed ranges
359 bool is_in_range = true;
360 for (int r = 0; r < options.main_resources.size(); ++r) {
361 if (bucket_from.lb[r] < range.first[r] ||
362 bucket_from.ub[r] > range.second[r]) {
363 is_in_range = false;
364 break;
365 }
366 }
367
368 if (is_in_range) {
369 size_t bit_pos = from * new_size + to;
370 size_t word_idx = bit_pos / 64;
371 if (word_idx < fixed_buckets_bitmap.size()) {
372 fixed_buckets_bitmap[word_idx] |=
373 (1ULL << (bit_pos % 64));
374 }
375 }
376 }
377 }
378 }
379
380 buckets_size = buckets.size();
381}

void BucketGraph::define_elementarity_sets ( )

Definition at line 348 of file BucketGraph.h.

348 {
349 size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
350
351 // Initialize elementarity sets
352 elementarity_bitmap.resize(num_nodes); // Bitmap for elementarity sets
353
354 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
355 // Each customer should appear in its own elementarity set
356 size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
357 elementarity_bitmap[i].resize(num_segments, 0);
358
359 // Mark the node itself in the elementarity set
360 size_t segment_self = i >> 6;
361 size_t bit_position_self = i & 63;
362 elementarity_bitmap[i][segment_self] |= (1ULL << bit_position_self);
363 }
364 }

◆ DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D, Stage S>
bool BucketGraph::DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets ( const Label *__restrict__ L,
int bucket,
const std::vector< double > &__restrict__ c_bar,
std::vector< uint64_t > &__restrict__ Bvisited,
const std::vector< std::vector< int > > &__restrict__ bucket_order )
inlinenoexcept

Determines if a label is dominated in component-wise smaller buckets.

This function checks if a given label is dominated by any other label in component-wise smaller buckets. The dominance is determined based on the cost and order of the buckets.

1038 UINT64_C(1) << 51, UINT64_C(1) << 52, UINT64_C(1) << 53,

1042 UINT64_C(1) << 63};
1043
1044 // Stack-based implementation with fixed size
1045 alignas(64) int stack_buffer[32]; // Aligned for better cache performance
1046 int stack_size = 1;
1047 stack_buffer[0] = bucket;
1048
1049 // SIMD-optimized dominance check using lambda to avoid function call
1050 // overhead
1051 const auto check_dominance = [&](const std::vector<Label *> &labels) {
1052#ifdef __AVX2__
1053 return check_dominance_against_vector<D, S>(L, labels, cut_storage,
1054 res_size);
1055#else
1056 const size_t n = labels.size();
1057 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
1058 if (is_dominated<D, S>(L, labels[i])) return true;
1059 }
1060 return false;
1061#endif
1062 };
1063
1064 // Main processing loop with optimizations
1065 do {
1066 const int current_bucket = stack_buffer[--stack_size];
1067
1068 // Optimize bit operations using pre-computed masks
1069 const size_t segment = static_cast<size_t>(current_bucket) >> 6;
1070 const uint64_t bit = bit_mask_lookup[current_bucket & 63];
1071 Bvisited[segment] |= bit;
1072
1073 // Early exit check with likely hint for better branch prediction
1074 if (likely(label_cost < c_bar[current_bucket] &&
1075 ::precedes<int>(bucket_order, current_bucket, b_L, uf))) {
1076 return false;
1077 }
1078
1079 // Check dominance only if necessary
1080 if (b_L != current_bucket) {
1081 auto &mother_bucket = buckets[current_bucket];
1082 int stat_n_dom = 0;
1083 if (mother_bucket.check_dominance(L, check_dominance, stat_n_dom)) {
1084 return true;
1085 }
1086 }
1087
1088 // Process neighbors with vectorization hints
1089 const auto &bucket_neighbors = Phi[current_bucket];
1090 const size_t n_neighbors = bucket_neighbors.size();
1091
1092#pragma GCC ivdep
1093 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_neighbors; ++i) {
1094 const int b_prime = bucket_neighbors[i];
1095 const size_t seg_prime = static_cast<size_t>(b_prime) >> 6;
1096 const uint64_t mask = bit_mask_lookup[b_prime & 63];
1097
1098 if (!(Bvisited[seg_prime] & mask)) {
1099 stack_buffer[stack_size++] = b_prime;
1100 }
1101 }
1102 } while (stack_size > 0);
1103
1104 return false;
1105}

template<Direction D, Stage S, ArcType A, Mutability M, Full F>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::Extend ( const std::conditional_t< M==Mutability::Mut, Label *, const Label * > L_prime,
const std::conditional_t< A==ArcType::Bucket, BucketArc, std::conditional_t< A==ArcType::Jump, JumpArc, Arc > > & gamma,

inlinenoexcept

Extends the label L_prime with the given BucketArc gamma.

562 {
563 static thread_local std::vector<double> new_resources;
564 if (new_resources.capacity() < options.resources.size()) {
565 new_resources.reserve(options.resources.size());
566 }
567 new_resources.resize(options.resources.size());
568
569 const int initial_node_id = L_prime->node_id;
570 auto initial_resources = L_prime->resources; // Make a copy as in original
571 const double initial_cost = L_prime->cost;
572
573 int node_id;
574 if constexpr (A == ArcType::Bucket) {
575 node_id =
576 assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets)[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
577 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Node) {
578 node_id = gamma.to;
579 } else {
580 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
581 node_id = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket].node_id;
582
583 // Update copy of resources for jump arcs
584 const auto &bucket = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket];
585 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
586 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
587 initial_resources[i] = std::max(
588 initial_resources[i], static_cast<double>(bucket.lb[i]));
589 } else {
590 initial_resources[i] = std::min(
591 initial_resources[i], static_cast<double>(bucket.ub[i]));
592 }
593 }
594 }
595
596 // Early rejection checks
597 if (node_id == L_prime->node_id ||
598 is_node_visited(L_prime->visited_bitmap, node_id)) {
599 return {};
600 }
601
602 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Eliminate) {
603 if ((D == Direction::Forward &&
604 fixed_arcs[initial_node_id][node_id] == 1) ||
605 (D == Direction::Backward &&
606 fixed_arcs[node_id][initial_node_id] == 1)) {
607 return {};
608 }
609 }
610
611 // Process resources and check feasibility
612 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
613 if (!process_all_resources<D>(new_resources, initial_resources, gamma,
614 nodes[node_id],
615 options.resources.size())) {
616 return {};
617 }
618 }
619
620 // Path size checks for PSTEP/TSP
621 if constexpr (F == Full::PSTEP || F == Full::TSP) {
622 int n_visited = 0;
623 for (const auto &bitmap : L_prime->visited_bitmap) {
624 n_visited += __builtin_popcountll(bitmap);
625 }
626
627 if (n_visited > options.max_path_size ||
628 (n_visited == options.max_path_size &&
629 node_id != options.end_depot)) {
630 return {};
631 }
632 }
633
634 const int to_bucket = get_bucket_number<D>(node_id, new_resources);
635
636 // Handle fixed buckets
637#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
638 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four && A != ArcType::Jump) {
639 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(L_prime->vertex, to_bucket)) {
640 if (depth > 1) return {};
641
642 static thread_local std::vector<Label *> label_vector;
643 label_vector.clear();
644
645 auto jump_arcs =
646 nodes[L_prime->node_id].template get_jump_arcs<D>(node_id);
647
648 for (const auto &jump_arc : jump_arcs) {
649 auto extended_labels =
650 Extend<D, S, ArcType::Jump, Mutability::Const, F>(
651 L_prime, jump_arc, depth + 1);
652 if (!extended_labels.empty()) {
653 label_vector.insert(label_vector.end(),
654 extended_labels.begin(),
655 extended_labels.end());
656 }
657 }
658 return label_vector;
659 }
660 }
661#endif
662
663#ifdef CUSTOM_COST
664 auto distance = getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
665 double new_cost =
666 cost_calculator.calculate_cost(initial_cost, // InitialCost
667 distance, // Distance
668 node_id, // NodeId
669 new_resources // Resources
670 );
671#else
672 double new_cost = initial_cost + getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
673#endif
674
675 const auto &VRPNode = nodes[node_id];
676
677 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
678 new_cost -= VRPNode.cost;
679 } else {
680 int n_visited = 0;
681 for (const auto &bitmap : L_prime->visited_bitmap) {
682 n_visited += __builtin_popcountll(bitmap);
683 }
684
685 if (n_visited > 1 && initial_node_id != options.depot) {
686 new_cost += pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(initial_node_id) +
687 pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(initial_node_id);
688 }
689 new_cost += -pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(node_id) +
690 pstep_duals.getArcDualValue(initial_node_id, node_id);
691 }
692
693 // Branching duals
694 if (!branching_duals->empty()) {
695 new_cost += (D == Direction::Forward)
696 ? branching_duals->getDual(initial_node_id, node_id)
697 : branching_duals->getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
698 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(node_id);
699 }
700
701 RCC_MODE_BLOCK(if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
702 new_cost -= (D == Direction::Forward)
703 ? arc_duals.getDual(initial_node_id, node_id)
704 : arc_duals.getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
705 })
706
707 // Handle Reverse case
708 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse) {
709 if (new_resources[options.main_resources[0]] <=
710 q_star[options.main_resources[0]]) {
711 return {};
712 }
713 auto &label_pool = assign_buckets<D>(label_pool_fw, label_pool_bw);
714 auto new_label = label_pool->acquire();
715 new_label->vertex = to_bucket;
716 return {new_label};
717 }
718
719 // Create and initialize new label
720 auto &label_pool = assign_buckets<D>(label_pool_fw, label_pool_bw);
721 auto new_label = label_pool->acquire();
722 new_label->initialize(to_bucket, new_cost, new_resources, node_id);
723 new_label->vertex = to_bucket;
724 new_label->real_cost =
725 L_prime->real_cost + getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
726
727 if constexpr (M == Mutability::Mut) {
728 new_label->parent = L_prime;
729 // L_prime->children.push_back(new_label);
730 }
731
732 // Handle visited bitmap
733 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
734 if constexpr (S != Stage::Enumerate) {
735 for (size_t i = 0; i < new_label->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
736 const uint64_t current_visited = L_prime->visited_bitmap[i];
737 if (current_visited) {
738 new_label->visited_bitmap[i] =
739 current_visited & neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][i];
740 }
741 }
742 }
743 } else {
744 new_label->visited_bitmap = L_prime->visited_bitmap;
745 }
746 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id);
747 new_label->nodes_covered = L_prime->nodes_covered;
748 new_label->nodes_covered.push_back(node_id);
749
750#if defined(SRC)
751 using simd_double = std::experimental::native_simd<double>;
752 using simd_abi = std::experimental::simd_abi::native<double>;
753 static constexpr size_t SIMD_WIDTH = simd_double::size();
754 static constexpr size_t SMALL_SIZE = 16;
755
756 new_label->SRCmap = L_prime->SRCmap;
757
758 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
759 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
760 const size_t segment = node_id >> 6;
761 const size_t bit_position = node_id & 63;
762 const uint64_t bit_mask = 1ULL << bit_position;
763
764 // Get active cuts once
765 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
766 const auto cut_size = cutter->activeSize();
767 const size_t simd_cut_size = cut_size - (cut_size % SIMD_WIDTH);
768
769 double total_cost_update = 0.0;
770
771#ifdef __AVX2__
772 if (cut_size <= SMALL_SIZE) {
773#endif
774
775 // Small size: direct processing of active cuts
776 for (const auto &active_cut : active_cuts) {
777 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
778 const size_t idx = active_cut.index;
779 const double dual_value = active_cut.dual_value;
780
781 if (cut.neighbors[segment] & bit_mask) {
782 if (cut.baseSet[segment] & bit_mask) {
783 const auto &multipliers = cut.p;
784 auto &src_map_value = new_label->SRCmap[idx];
785 src_map_value +=
786 multipliers.num[cut.baseSetOrder[node_id]];
787 if (src_map_value >= multipliers.den) {
788 src_map_value -= multipliers.den;
789 total_cost_update -= dual_value;
790 }
791 }
792 } else {
793 new_label->SRCmap[idx] = 0.0;
794 }
795 }
796
797#ifdef __AVX2__
798 } else {
799 // SIMD processing for larger sizes
800 alignas(64) std::array<double, SIMD_WIDTH> dual_values;
801 alignas(64) std::array<size_t, SIMD_WIDTH> indices;
802 alignas(64) std::array<const Cut *, SIMD_WIDTH> cut_ptrs;
803
804 for (size_t base_idx = 0; base_idx < simd_cut_size;
805 base_idx += SIMD_WIDTH) {
806 // Prepare SIMD batch
807 for (size_t j = 0; j < SIMD_WIDTH; ++j) {
808 const auto &active_cut = active_cuts[base_idx + j];
809 dual_values[j] = active_cut.dual_value;
810 indices[j] = active_cut.index;
811 cut_ptrs[j] = active_cut.cut_ptr;
812 }
813
814 simd_double duals_vec = load_simd_values(
815 std::span<const double>(dual_values.data(), SIMD_WIDTH), 0);
816
817 // Process SIMD batch
818 for (size_t j = 0; j < SIMD_WIDTH; ++j) {
819 const auto &cut = *cut_ptrs[j];
820 const size_t idx = indices[j];
821
822 if (cut.neighbors[segment] & bit_mask) {
823 if (cut.baseSet[segment] & bit_mask) {
824 const auto &multipliers = cut.p;
825 auto &src_map_value = new_label->SRCmap[idx];
826 src_map_value +=
827 multipliers.num[cut.baseSetOrder[node_id]];
828 if (src_map_value >= multipliers.den) {
829 src_map_value -= multipliers.den;
830 total_cost_update -= dual_values[j];
831 }
832 }
833 } else {
834 new_label->SRCmap[idx] = 0.0;
835 }
836 }
837 }
838
839 // Handle remaining active cuts
840 for (size_t i = simd_cut_size; i < cut_size; ++i) {
841 const auto &active_cut = active_cuts[i];
842 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
843 const size_t idx = active_cut.index;
844 const double dual_value = active_cut.dual_value;
845
846 if (cut.neighbors[segment] & bit_mask) {
847 if (cut.baseSet[segment] & bit_mask) {
848 const auto &multipliers = cut.p;
849 auto &src_map_value = new_label->SRCmap[idx];
850 src_map_value +=
851 multipliers.num[cut.baseSetOrder[node_id]];
852 if (src_map_value >= multipliers.den) {
853 src_map_value -= multipliers.den;
854 total_cost_update -= dual_value;
855 }
856 }
857 } else {
858 new_label->SRCmap[idx] = 0.0;
859 }
860 }
861 }
862#endif
863
864 new_label->cost += total_cost_update;
865 }
866#endif
867
868 // Return the array (as Label**)
869 return std::vector<Label *>{new_label};
870}

◆ forbidCycle()

void BucketGraph::forbidCycle ( const std::vector< int > & cycle,
bool aggressive )

Forbids a cycle in the bucket graph.

This function takes a vector representing a cycle in the graph and forbids the edges corresponding to the cycle. If the 'aggressive' flag is set to true, it also forbids additional edges between the vertices of the cycle.

Definition at line 435 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

435 {
436 for (size_t i = 0; i < cycle.size() - 1; ++i) {
437 int v1 = cycle[i];
438 int v2 = cycle[i + 1];
439
440 // Update the bitmap to forbid v2 in the neighborhood of v1
441 size_t segment = v2 >> 6;
442 size_t bit_position = v2 & 63;
443 neighborhoods_bitmap[v1][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
444
445 if (aggressive) {
446 segment = v1 >> 6;
447 bit_position = v1 & 63;
448 neighborhoods_bitmap[v2][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
449 }
450 }
451}

◆ generate_arcs() [1/2]

void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in both forward and backward directions in parallel.

This function uses OpenMP to parallelize the generation of arcs in both forward and backward directions. It performs the following steps for each direction:

  • Calls the generate_arcs function template with the appropriate direction.
  • Clears and resizes the Phi vector for the respective direction.
  • Computes the Phi values for each bucket and stores them in the Phi vector.
  • Calls the SCC_handler function template with the appropriate direction.

The forward direction operations are performed in one OpenMP section, and the backward direction operations are performed in another OpenMP section.

Definition at line 787 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

787 {
788 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
789 work, bi_sched,
790 SECTION {
791 // Task for Forward Direction
792 generate_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
793 Phi_fw.clear();
794 Phi_fw.resize(fw_buckets_size);
795 for (int i = 0; i < fw_buckets_size; ++i) {
796 Phi_fw[i] = computePhi(i, true);
797 }
798 SCC_handler<Direction::Forward>();
799 },
800 SECTION {
801 // Task for Backward Direction
802 generate_arcs<Direction::Backward>();
803 Phi_bw.clear();
804 Phi_bw.resize(bw_buckets_size);
805 for (int i = 0; i < bw_buckets_size; ++i) {
806 Phi_bw[i] = computePhi(i, false);
807 }
808 SCC_handler<Direction::Backward>();
809 });
810}

◆ generate_arcs() [2/2]

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in the bucket graph based on the specified direction.

Generates arcs in the bucket graph based on the specified direction.

Definition at line 390 of file BucketUtils.h.

390 {
391 auto buckets_mutex = std::mutex();
392
393 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
394 fw_bucket_graph.clear();
395 } else {
396 bw_bucket_graph.clear();
397 }
398
399 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
400 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
401 auto &num_buckets_index =
402 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
403 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
404 // Pre-compute intervals for all nodes
405 std::vector<std::vector<double>> node_intervals(nodes.size());
406 for (size_t node_id = 0; node_id < nodes.size(); ++node_id) {
407 const auto &node = nodes[node_id];
408 node_intervals[node_id].resize(options.resources.size());
409 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
410 node_intervals[node_id][r] =
411 (node.ub[r] - node.lb[r]) / bucket_splits[node_id];
412 }
413 }
414
415 // Clear buckets
416 for (auto &bucket : buckets) {
417 bucket.clear();
418 bucket.clear_arcs(D == Direction::Forward);
419 }
420
421 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &node, int from_bucket,
422 std::vector<double> &res_inc,
423 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> &local_arcs) {
424 const auto arcs = node.get_arcs<D>();
425 const auto &node_interval = node_intervals[node.id];
426
427 for (const auto &arc : arcs) {
428 // print node_id
429 const auto &next_node = nodes[arc.to];
430 if (node.id == next_node.id) continue;
431
432 const auto &next_node_interval = node_intervals[arc.to];
433 const auto travel_cost = getcij(node.id, next_node.id);
434 const double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
435
436 // Pre-calculate resource increments
437 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); r++) {
438 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
439 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
440 res_inc[r] += travel_cost;
441 }
442 }
443
444 for (int j = 0; j < num_buckets[next_node.id]; ++j) {
445 const int to_bucket = j + num_buckets_index[next_node.id];
446 if (from_bucket == to_bucket ||
447 is_bucket_fixed<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket)) {
448 continue;
449 }
450
451 bool valid_arc = true;
452
453 // Resource bounds check
454 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
455 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size() && valid_arc; ++r) {
456 if (buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r] >
457 next_node.ub[r]) {
458 valid_arc = false;
459 } else {
460 double max_calc = std::max(
461 buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r],
462 next_node.lb[r]);
463 if (max_calc < buckets[to_bucket].lb[r] ||
464 max_calc >= buckets[to_bucket].lb[r] +
465 next_node_interval[r] +
466 numericutils::eps) {
467 valid_arc = false;
468 }
469 }
470 }
471 } else {
472 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size() && valid_arc; ++r) {
473 if (buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r] <
474 next_node.lb[r]) {
475 valid_arc = false;
476 } else {
477 double min_calc = std::min(
478 buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r],
479 next_node.ub[r]);
480 if (min_calc > buckets[to_bucket].ub[r] ||
481 min_calc <= buckets[to_bucket].ub[r] -
482 next_node_interval[r] -
483 numericutils::eps) {
484 valid_arc = false;
485 }
486 }
487 }
488 }
489
490 if (valid_arc) {
491 local_arcs.emplace_back(from_bucket, to_bucket);
492 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(buckets_mutex);
493 add_arc<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket, res_inc, cost_inc);
494 buckets[from_bucket].template add_bucket_arc<D>(
495 from_bucket, to_bucket, res_inc, cost_inc, false);
496 }
497 }
498 }
499 };
500
501 const unsigned int thread_count = std::thread::hardware_concurrency() / 2;
502 exec::static_thread_pool pool(thread_count);
503 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
504
505 std::vector<int> tasks(nodes.size());
506 std::iota(tasks.begin(), tasks.end(), 0);
507
508 const int chunk_size = 10;
509 auto bulk_sender = stdexec::bulk(
510 stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size,
511 [this, &tasks, &num_buckets, &num_buckets_index, &add_arcs_for_node,
512 chunk_size](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
513 const size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * chunk_size;
514 const size_t end_idx =
515 std::min(start_idx + chunk_size, tasks.size());
516
517 std::vector<double> res_inc(options.resources.size());
518 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> local_arcs;
519 local_arcs.reserve(chunk_size *
520 100); // Estimate average arcs per chunk
521
522 for (size_t task_idx = start_idx; task_idx < end_idx; ++task_idx) {
523 const int node_id = tasks[task_idx];
524 const auto &node = nodes[node_id];
525
526 for (int i = 0; i < num_buckets[node.id]; ++i) {
527 const int from_bucket = i + num_buckets_index[node.id];
528 add_arcs_for_node(node, from_bucket, res_inc, local_arcs);
529 }
530 }
531 });
532
533 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
534 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
535}

1233
1234 // Retrieve all arcs associated with the current node
1235 for (const auto &arc : node.get_arcs<Direction::Forward>()) {

1244 }
1245
1246 // Add the node's arcs to the adjacency list if it has any outgoing
1247 // edges

1249 adjacency_list[node.id] = arcs;
1250 }
1251 }

1253 return adjacency_list;

◆ get_best_label()

template<Direction D>
Label * BucketGraph::get_best_label ( const std::vector< int > & topological_order,
const std::vector< double > & c_bar,
const std::vector< std::vector< int > > & sccs )

Retrieves the best label from the bucket graph based on the given topological order, c_bar values, and strongly connected components.

This function iterates through the given topological order and for each component, it retrieves the labels from the corresponding buckets in the bucket graph. It then compares the cost of each label and keeps track of the label with the lowest cost. The best label, along with its associated bucket, is returned.

Definition at line 549 of file BucketUtils.h.

551 {
552 double best_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
553 Label *best_label = nullptr; // Ensure this is initialized
554 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
555
556 for (const int component_index : topological_order) {
557 const auto &component_buckets = sccs[component_index];
558
559 for (const int bucket : component_buckets) {
560 const auto &label = buckets[bucket].get_best_label();
561 if (!label) continue;
562 // print label->cost
563 if (label->cost < best_cost) {
564 best_cost = label->cost;
565 best_label = label;
566 }
567 }
568 }
569
570 return best_label;
571}

102 }

◆ get_opposite_bucket_number()

template<Direction D>
int BucketGraph::get_opposite_bucket_number ( int current_bucket_index,
std::vector< double > & inc )

Get the opposite bucket number for a given bucket index.

This function retrieves the opposite bucket number based on the current bucket index and the specified direction. It determines the node and bounds of the current bucket, then calculates the opposite bucket index using the appropriate direction.

Definition at line 925 of file BucketUtils.h.

926 {
927 // TODO: adjust to multi-resource case
928 auto &current_bucket = (D == Direction::Forward)
929 ? fw_buckets[current_bucket_index]
930 : bw_buckets[current_bucket_index];
931 int &node = current_bucket.node_id;
932 auto &theNode = nodes[node];
933
934 // Find the opposite bucket using the appropriate direction
935 int opposite_bucket_index = -1;
936 std::vector<double> reference_point(options.main_resources.size());
937 for (int r = 0; r < options.main_resources.size(); ++r) {
938 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
939 reference_point[r] = std::max(inc[r], theNode.lb[r]);
940 } else {
941 reference_point[r] = std::min(inc[r], theNode.ub[r]);
942 }
943 }
944 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
945 opposite_bucket_index =
946 get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(node, reference_point);
947 } else {
948 opposite_bucket_index =
949 get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(node, reference_point);
950 }
951
952 return opposite_bucket_index;
953}

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::heuristic_fixing ( )

Applies heuristic fixing to the current solution.

This function modifies the current solution based on the heuristic fixing strategy using the provided vector of values.

1030 // Stage 3 fixing heuristic
1031 reset_pool();
1032 reset_fixed();
1033 common_initialization();
1034
1035 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size(),
1036 std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1037 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size(),
1038 std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1039
1040 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Two, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar,
1041 backward_cbar);

1044 std::vector<std::vector<Label *>> bw_labels_map(nodes.size());
1045
1046 auto group_labels = [&](auto &buckets, auto &labels_map) {
1047 for (auto &bucket : buckets) {
1048 for (auto label : bucket.get_labels()) {
1049 labels_map[label->node_id].push_back(
1050 label); // Directly index using node_id
1051 }
1052 }
1053 };
1054
1055 // Create tasks for forward and backward labels grouping
1056 auto forward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1057 group_labels(fw_buckets, fw_labels_map);
1058 });
1059 auto backward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1060 group_labels(bw_buckets, bw_labels_map);
1061 });
1062
1063 // Execute the tasks in parallel
1064 auto work =
1065 stdexec::when_all(std::move(forward_task), std::move(backward_task));
1066
1067 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
1068
1069 auto num_fixes = 0;
1070 // Function to find the minimum cost label in a vector of labels
1071 auto find_min_cost_label =
1072 [](const std::vector<Label *> &labels) -> const Label * {
1073 return *std::min_element(
1074 labels.begin(), labels.end(),
1075 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
1076 };
1077 for (const auto &node_I : nodes) {
1078 const auto &fw_labels = fw_labels_map[node_I.id];
1079 if (fw_labels.empty()) continue; // Skip if no labels for this node_id
1080
1081 for (const auto &node_J : nodes) {
1082 if (node_I.id == node_J.id)
1083 continue; // Compare based on id (or other key field)
1084 const auto &bw_labels = bw_labels_map[node_J.id];
1085 if (bw_labels.empty())
1086 continue; // Skip if no labels for this node_id
1087
1088 const Label *min_fw_label = find_min_cost_label(fw_labels);
1089 const Label *min_bw_label = find_min_cost_label(bw_labels);
1090
1091 if (!min_fw_label || !min_bw_label) continue;
1092
1093 const VRPNode &L_last_node = nodes[min_fw_label->node_id];
1094 auto cost = getcij(min_fw_label->node_id, min_bw_label->node_id);
1095
1096 bool violated = false;
1097 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1098 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
1099 if (min_fw_label->resources[TIME_INDEX] + cost +
1100 L_last_node.duration >
1101 min_bw_label->resources[TIME_INDEX]) {
1102 violated = true;
1103 break;
1104 }
1105 } else {
1106 if (min_fw_label->resources[r] +
1107 L_last_node.consumption[r] >
1108 min_bw_label->resources[r]) {
1109 violated = true;
1110 break;
1111 }
1112 }
1113 }
1114
1115 if (violated) continue;
1116
1117 if (min_fw_label->cost + cost + min_bw_label->cost > gap) {
1118 fixed_arcs[node_I.id][node_J.id] = 1; // Index with node ids
1119 num_fixes++;
1120 }
1121 }
1122 }
1123}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ initInfo()

void BucketGraph::initInfo ( )
static

Prints the configuration information of the BucketGraph.

This function outputs the configuration details including resource size, number of clients, and maximum SRC cuts. It also conditionally prints whether RIH, RCC, and SRC are enabled or disabled based on the preprocessor directives.

The output format is as follows:

+-------------------------------—+ | CONFIGURATION INFO | +-------------------------------—+ Resources: <R_SIZE> Number of Clients: <N_SIZE> Maximum SRC cuts: <MAX_SRC_CUTS> RIH: <enabled/disabled> RCC: <enabled/disabled> SRC: <enabled/disabled> +-------------------------------—+

Definition at line 889 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

889 {
890 // Print header
891 fmt::print("\n+----------------------------------+\n");
892 fmt::print("| CONFIGURATION INFO |\n");
893 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
894
895 // Print Resource size
896 fmt::print("Resources: {}\n", R_SIZE);
897 fmt::print("Number of Clients: {}\n", N_SIZE - 2);
898
899 // Conditional configuration (RIH enabled/disabled)
900#ifdef RIH
901 fmt::print("RIH: enabled\n");
902#else
903 fmt::print("RIH: disabled\n");
904#endif
905#ifdef RCC
906 fmt::print("RCC: enabled\n");
907#else
908 fmt::print("RCC: disabled\n");
909#endif
910#ifdef SRC
911 fmt::print("SRC: enabled\n");
912#else
913 fmt::print("SRC: disabled\n");
914#endif
915 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
916
917 fmt::print("\n");
918}

◆ is_dominated()

template<Direction D, Stage S>
bool BucketGraph::is_dominated ( const Label * new_label,
const Label * labels )
inlinenoexcept

Checks if a label is dominated by a new label based on cost and resource conditions.

Definition at line 878 of file BucketSolve.h.

879 {
880 // Extract resources for the new label and the comparison label
881 const auto &new_resources = new_label->resources;
882 const auto &label_resources = label->resources;
883
884 {
885 // Simple cost check: if the comparison label has a higher cost, it
886 // is not dominated
887 if (label->cost > new_label->cost) {
888 return false;
889 }
890 }
891
892 // Check resource conditions based on the direction (Forward or
893 // Backward)
894 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
895 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
896 // In Forward direction: the comparison label must not have more
897 // resources than the new label
898 if (label_resources[i] > new_resources[i]) {
899 return false;
900 }
901 } else if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
902 // In Backward direction: the comparison label must not have
903 // fewer resources than the new label
904 if (label_resources[i] < new_resources[i]) {
905 return false;
906 }
907 }
908 }
909
910 // TODO:: check again this unreachable dominance
911#ifndef UNREACHABLE_DOMINANCE
912 // Check visited nodes (bitmap comparison) for Stages 3, 4, and
913 // Enumerate
914 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Four ||
915 S == Stage::Enumerate) {
916 // Iterate through the visited bitmap and ensure that the new label
917 // visits all nodes that the comparison label visits
918 for (size_t i = 0; i < label->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
919 // If the comparison label visits a node that the new label does
920 // not, it is not dominated
921 if (((label->visited_bitmap[i] & new_label->visited_bitmap[i]) ^
922 label->visited_bitmap[i]) != 0) {
923 return false;
924 }
925 }
926 }
927#else
928 // Unreachable dominance logic: check visited and unreachable nodes in
929 // Stages 3, 4, and Enumerate
930 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Four ||
931 S == Stage::Enumerate) {
932 for (size_t i = 0; i < label->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
933 // Combine visited and unreachable nodes in the comparison
934 // label's bitmap
935 auto combined_label_bitmap =
936 label->visited_bitmap[i] | label->unreachable_bitmap[i];
937 // Ensure the new label visits all nodes that the comparison
938 // label (or its unreachable nodes) visits
939 if ((combined_label_bitmap & ~new_label->visited_bitmap[i]) != 0) {
940 return false;
941 }
942 }
943 }
944#endif
945
946#ifdef SRC
947 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
948 double sumSRC = 0;
949
950 const auto &SRCDuals = cut_storage->SRCDuals;
951 if (!SRCDuals.empty()) {
952 const auto &labelSRCMap = label->SRCmap;
953 const auto &newLabelSRCMap = new_label->SRCmap;
954 for (size_t i = 0; i < SRCDuals.size(); ++i) {
955 const auto &den = cut_storage->getCut(i).p.den;
956 const auto labelMod = labelSRCMap[i]; // % den;
957 const auto newLabelMod = newLabelSRCMap[i]; // % den;
958 if (labelMod > newLabelMod) {
959 sumSRC += SRCDuals[i];
960 }
961 }
962 }
963 if (label->cost - sumSRC > new_label->cost) {
964 return false;
965 }
966 }
967#endif
968
969 // If all conditions are met, return true, indicating that the new label
970 // is dominated by the comparison label
971 return true;
972}

static bool BucketGraph::is_node_unreachable ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,

inlinestatic

Checks if a node is unreachable based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a node, identified by its node_id, is unreachable by checking a specific bit in a bitmap. The bitmap is represented as an array of 64-bit unsigned integers.

◆ is_node_visited()

static bool BucketGraph::is_node_visited ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node has been visited based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a specific node, identified by node_id, has been visited by checking the corresponding bit in a bitmap array. The bitmap is an array of 64-bit unsigned integers, where each bit represents the visited status of a node.
