double BucketGraph::knapsackBound ( const Label * l)

◆ labeling_algorithm()

template<Direction D, Stage S, Full F>
std::vector< double > BucketGraph::labeling_algorithm ( )

Performs the labeling algorithm on the BucketGraph.

Definition at line 222 of file BucketSolve.h.

222 {
223 // Get references to avoid repeated lookups
224 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
225 auto &ordered_sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_ordered_sccs, bw_ordered_sccs);
226 auto &topological_order =
227 assign_buckets<D>(fw_topological_order, bw_topological_order);
228 auto &sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs, bw_sccs);
229 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
230 auto &c_bar = assign_buckets<D>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
231 auto &n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(n_fw_labels, n_bw_labels);
232 auto &sorted_sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs_sorted, bw_sccs_sorted);
233 auto &n_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
234 auto &stat_n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
235 auto &stat_n_dom = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
236
237 n_labels = 0;
238
239 // Pre-calculate segment info using bit shift for better performance
240 const size_t n_segments = (n_buckets + 63) >> 6;
241 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments);
242
243 // Use small vector optimization for new labels
244 std::vector<Label *> new_labels;
245 const auto q_star_value = q_star[options.main_resources[0]];
246
247 // Process SCCs in topological order
248 for (const auto &scc_index : topological_order) {
249 bool all_ext;
250 do {
251 all_ext = true;
252
253 // Process buckets in sorted order within SCC
254 for (const auto bucket : sorted_sccs[scc_index]) {
255 // Cache bucket labels reference
256 const auto &bucket_labels = buckets[bucket].get_labels();
257 std::span<Label *const> labels_span{bucket_labels};
258
259 for (Label *label : labels_span) {
260 if (label->is_extended) continue;
261
262 // Early resource check for partial solutions
263 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP && F != Full::TSP) {
264 if constexpr (F == Full::Partial) {
265 const auto main_resource =
266 label->resources[options.main_resources[0]];
267 if (((D == Direction::Forward) &&
268 (main_resource > q_star_value)) ||
269 ((D == Direction::Backward) &&
270 (main_resource <= q_star_value))) {
271 label->set_extended(true);
272 continue;
273 }
274 }
275 }
276
277 // Efficient visited bucket clearing
278 if (n_segments <= 8) {
279 std::fill_n(Bvisited.begin(), n_segments, 0);
280 } else {
281 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0,
282 n_segments * sizeof(uint64_t));
283 }
284
285 // Check dominance in smaller buckets
286 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
287 if (DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets<D, S>(
288 label, bucket, c_bar, Bvisited, ordered_sccs)) {
289 buckets[label->vertex].lazy_flush(label);
290 label->set_extended(true);
291 continue;
292 }
293 }
294
295 // Process arcs for current label
296 const auto &node_arcs =
297 nodes[label->node_id].template get_arcs<D>(scc_index);
298
299 for (const auto &arc : node_arcs) {
300 new_labels.clear();
301
302 auto extended_labels =
303 Extend<D, S, ArcType::Node, Mutability::Mut, F>(
304 label, arc);
305 if (extended_labels.empty()) {
306#ifdef UNREACHABLE_DOMINANCE
307 set_node_unreachable(label->unreachable_bitmap,
308 arc.to);
309#endif
310 continue;
311 }
312
313 // Process each new label
314 for (Label *new_label : extended_labels) {
315 ++stat_n_labels;
316
317 const int to_bucket = new_label->vertex;
318 auto &mother_bucket = buckets[to_bucket];
319 const auto &to_bucket_labels =
320 mother_bucket.get_labels();
321
322 bool dominated = false;
323 if constexpr (S == Stage::One) {
324 // Stage One processing with early exits
325 for (auto *existing_label : to_bucket_labels) {
326 if (label->cost < existing_label->cost) {
327 mother_bucket.remove_label(
328 existing_label);
329 } else {
330 dominated = true;
331 break;
332 }
333 }
334 } else {
335 // SIMD dominance check
336 auto check_dominance_in_bucket =
337 [&](const std::vector<Label *> &labels) {
338#ifdef __AVX2__
339 return check_dominance_against_vector<
340 D, S>(new_label, labels,
341 cut_storage,
342 options.resources.size());
343#else
344 for (auto *existing_label : labels) {
345 if (is_dominated<D, S>(
346 new_label,
347 existing_label)) {
348 ++stat_n_dom;
349 return true;
350 }
351 }
352 return false;
353#endif
354 };
355
356 dominated = mother_bucket.check_dominance(
357 new_label, check_dominance_in_bucket,
358 stat_n_dom);
359 }
360
361 if (!dominated) {
362 if constexpr (S != Stage::Enumerate) {
363 for (auto *existing_label :
364 to_bucket_labels) {
365 if (is_dominated<D, S>(existing_label,
366 new_label)) {
367 buckets[to_bucket].remove_label(
368 existing_label);
369 }
370 }
371 }
372
373 ++n_labels;
374#ifdef SORTED_LABELS
375 mother_bucket.add_sorted_label(new_label);
376#elif LIMITED_BUCKETS
377 mother_bucket.sorted_label(new_label,
378 BUCKET_CAPACITY);
379#else
380 mother_bucket.add_label(new_label);
381#endif
382 all_ext = false;
383 }
384 }
385 }
386 label->set_extended(true);
387 }
388 buckets[bucket].flush();
389 }
390 } while (!all_ext);
391
392 // Update c_bar values efficiently
393 for (const int bucket : sorted_sccs[scc_index]) {
394 double min_c_bar = buckets[bucket].get_cb();
395 for (const auto phi_bucket : Phi[bucket]) {
396 min_c_bar = std::min(min_c_bar, c_bar[phi_bucket]);
397 }
398 c_bar[bucket] = min_c_bar;
399 }
400 }
401
402 // Store best label
403 if (Label *best_label = get_best_label<D>(topological_order, c_bar, sccs)) {
404 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
405 fw_best_label = best_label;
406 } else {
407 bw_best_label = best_label;
408 }
409 }
410
411 return c_bar;
412}
Stage::One
@ One

◆ mono_initialization()

void BucketGraph::mono_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph for the mono-directional case.

Definition at line 589 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

589 {
590 // Clear previous data
591 merged_labels.clear();
592 merged_labels.reserve(100);
593 fw_c_bar.clear();
594 bw_c_bar.clear();
595
596 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(fw_buckets_size + 1, 0);
597 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
598
599 // Resize cost vectors to match the number of buckets
600 fw_c_bar.resize(fw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
601 bw_c_bar.resize(bw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
602
603 auto &num_buckets =
604 assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
605 auto &num_bucket_index = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(
606 num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
607
608 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
609 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
610 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
611
612 auto &VRPNode = nodes[0]; // Example for the first node
613
614 // Calculate base intervals and total ranges for each resource dimension
615 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
616 base_intervals[r] =
617 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
618 }
619
620 // Clear forward and backward buckets
621 for (auto b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); b++) {
622 fw_buckets[b].clear();
623 bw_buckets[b].clear();
624 }
625
626 // Initialize forward buckets (generic for multiple dimensions)
627 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
628
629 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
630 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
631
632 // Initialize interval_starts to lower bounds and interval_ends to upper
633 // bounds
634 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
635 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
636 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
637 }
638 int offset_fw = 0;
639 int offset_bw = 0;
640
641 // Lambda to handle forward and backward direction initialization across
642 // combinations
643 auto initialize_intervals_combinations = [&](bool is_forward) {
644 int &offset = is_forward ? offset_fw : offset_bw;
645 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
646 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
647 const auto &depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
648 // print depot_id
649 int calculated_index_base =
650 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot]
651 : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
652
653 std::vector<int> current_pos(
654 num_intervals,
655 0); // Tracks current position in each interval dimension
656 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
657
658 // Recursive lambda to generate all combinations of intervals
659 std::function<void(int)> generate_combinations = [&](int depth) {
660 if (depth == num_intervals) {
661 // All dimensions processed, now initialize for the current
662 // combination
663 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
664 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
665
666 // Set interval_bounds to current combination of interval starts
667 // or ends
668 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
669 interval_bounds[r] =
670 is_forward
671 ? interval_starts[r] +
672 current_pos[r] *
673 roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r])
674 : interval_ends[r] -
675 current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(
676 base_intervals[r]);
677 }
678
679 // print interval
680 // Initialize depot with the current interval boundaries
681 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds,
682 depot_id);
683 depot->is_extended = false;
684 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(depot_id);
685 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(depot_id);
686 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
687 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(
688 depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->SRCDuals.size(), 0);)
689 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
690 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
691
692 offset++; // Increment offset for each combination
693 return;
694 }
695
696 // Loop through each interval in the current dimension
697 for (int k = 0; k < intervals[depth].interval; ++k) {
698 current_pos[depth] = k;
699 generate_combinations(depth + 1);
700 }
701 };
702
703 // Start generating combinations from depth 0
704 generate_combinations(0);
705 };
706
707 // Call the lambda for both forward and backward directions, ensuring all
708 // combinations are processed
709 initialize_intervals_combinations(true); // Forward direction
710}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ ObtainJumpBucketArcs()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::ObtainJumpBucketArcs ( )

Obtains jump bucket arcs for the BucketGraph.

This function iterates over each bucket in the BucketGraph and adds jump arcs to the set of buckets. It checks if each arc exists in the given Gamma vector for the current bucket. If an arc exists, it adds the corresponding bucket to the set of buckets to add jump arcs to. It then removes the non-component-wise minimal buckets from the set. Finally, it adds jump arcs from the current bucket to each bucket in the set.

Definition at line 340 of file BucketJump.h.

340 {
341 // Assign forward or backward buckets, fixed buckets, bucket indices, and
342 // Phi (adjacency list) based on direction
343 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
344 auto &fixed_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets, bw_fixed_buckets);
345 auto &num_buckets_index =
346 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
347 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
348 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
349 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
350
351 int arc_counter = 0; // Counter for jump arcs added
352 int missing_counter = 0; // Counter for missing paths
353
354 // iterate over all nodes and clear jump arcs
355 for (auto &node : nodes) {
356 node.clear_jump_arcs<D>();
357 }
358
359 // Loop through all buckets
360 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
361 std::vector<int> B_bar; // Temporary storage for valid bucket indices
362
363 // Cache the current bucket's node ID and arcs
364 const int current_node_id = buckets[b].node_id;
365 const auto &arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
366 const auto &original_arcs =
367 nodes[current_node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
368
369 if (arcs.empty()) {
370 continue;
371 } // Skip if no arcs in the current bucket
372
373 // Process each original arc for the current node
374 for (const auto &orig_arc : original_arcs) {
375 const int from_node = orig_arc.from;
376 const int to_node = orig_arc.to;
377 bool have_path = false;
378
379 // Check if the path exists in the current bucket arcs
380 for (const auto &gamma : arcs) {
381 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma.from_bucket, gamma.to_bucket)) {
382 continue;
383 }
384
385 const int from_node_b = buckets[gamma.from_bucket].node_id;
386 const int to_node_b = buckets[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
387
388 if (from_node == from_node_b && to_node == to_node_b) {
389 have_path = true;
390 break; // Path found, exit early
391 }
392 }
393
394 // If no path was found, find a jump arc
395 if (!have_path) {
396 ++missing_counter; // Increment missing path counter
397
398 // Cache the starting bucket and the number of buckets for this
399 // node
400 const int start_bucket = num_buckets_index[current_node_id];
401 const int node_buckets = num_buckets[current_node_id];
402
403 auto process_bucket = [&](int b, int b_prime) {
404 // Check if b_prime is adjacent to b in Phi
405 const auto &phi_b_prime = Phi[b_prime];
406 if (std::find(phi_b_prime.begin(), phi_b_prime.end(), b) ==
407 phi_b_prime.end()) {
408 return; // Not adjacent, skip
409 }
410
411 // Check arcs in the adjacent bucket b_prime
412 const auto &arcs_prime =
413 buckets[b_prime].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
414 for (const auto &gamma_prime : arcs_prime) {
415 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma_prime.from_bucket,
416 gamma_prime.to_bucket)) {
417 continue;
418 }
419
420 const int from_node_prime =
421 buckets[gamma_prime.from_bucket].node_id;
422 const int to_node_prime =
423 buckets[gamma_prime.to_bucket].node_id;
424
425 // If the arc matches, add a jump arc
426 if (from_node == from_node_prime &&
427 to_node == to_node_prime) {
428 B_bar.push_back(
429 b_prime); // Add the valid bucket to B_bar
430
431 // Wrap the resource increment in a vector
432 const std::vector<double> res =
433 gamma_prime.resource_increment;
434 const double cost = gamma_prime.cost_increment;
435
436 buckets[b].template add_jump_arc<D>(b, b_prime, res,
437 cost);
438 nodes[buckets[b].node_id].template add_jump_arc<D>(
439 b, b_prime, res, cost, to_node);
440 ++arc_counter; // Increment the jump arc counter
441 }
442 }
443 };
444
445 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
446 // Forward direction: incrementing buckets
447 for (int b_prime = b + 1;
448 b_prime < start_bucket + node_buckets; ++b_prime) {
449 process_bucket(b, b_prime);
450 }
451 } else {
452 // Backward direction: decrementing buckets
453 // for (int b_prime = b - 1; b_prime >= start_bucket;
454 // --b_prime) { process_bucket(b, b_prime); }
455 for (int b_prime = b + 1;
456 b_prime < start_bucket + node_buckets; ++b_prime) {
457 process_bucket(b, b_prime);
458 }
459 }
460 }
461 }
462 }
463 CONDITIONAL(D, print_info("[Fw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter),
464 print_info("[Bw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter));
465}
CONDITIONAL
#define CONDITIONAL(D, FW_ACTION, BW_ACTION)

◆ print_statistics()

void BucketGraph::print_statistics ( )

Prints the statistics of the bucket graph.

This function outputs a formatted table displaying various metrics related to the bucket graph. The table includes headers and values for forward and backward labels, as well as dominance checks. The output is color-coded for better readability:

  • Bold blue for metric names
  • Green for values (backward labels)
  • Reset color to default after each line

The table structure: +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Metric | Forward | Backward | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Labels | <stat_n_labels_fw> | <stat_n_labels_bw> | | Dominance Check | <stat_n_dom_fw> | <stat_n_dom_bw> | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+

Note: The actual values for the metrics (e.g., stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw, etc.) should be provided by the corresponding member variables or functions.

Definition at line 747 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

747 {
748 const char *blue_bold = "\033[1;34m"; // Bold blue for metrics
749 const char *green = "\033[32m"; // Green for values (backward labels)
750 const char *reset = "\033[0m"; // Reset color
751
752 // Print table header with horizontal line and separators
753 fmt::print(
754 "\n+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
755 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Metric", reset,
756 " Forward", " Backward");
757 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
758
759 // Print labels for forward and backward with bold blue metric
760 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Labels", reset,
761 stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
762
763 // Print dominated forward and backward labels with bold blue metric
764 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold,
765 " Dominance Check", reset, stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
766
767 // Print the final horizontal line
768 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
769
770 fmt::print("\n");
771}

◆ process_all_resources()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_all_resources ( std::vector< double > & new_resources,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t N )

Processes all resources by iterating through them and applying constraints.

This function recursively processes each resource in the new_resources vector by calling process_resource for each index from I to N-1. If any resource processing fails (i.e., process_resource returns false), the function returns false immediately. If all resources are processed successfully, the function returns true.

Definition at line 117 of file BucketRes.h.

120 {
121 static constexpr ResourceProcessor processors[] = {
122 &process_non_disposable<D>, &process_disposable<D>,
123 &process_binary<D>, &process_mtw<D>,
124 &process_battery<D>, &process_recharge_count<D>,
125 &process_recharge_time<D>};
126
127 for (size_t i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
128 if (!processors[options.resource_type[i]](
129 new_resources[i], initial_resources[i],
130 gamma.resource_increment[i], theNode, options)) {
131 return false;
132 }
133 }
134 return true;
135}

◆ process_resource()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_resource ( double & new_resource,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t I )
constexpr

Processes a resource based on its disposability type and direction.

This function updates the new_resource value based on the initial resources, the increment provided by gamma, and the constraints defined by theNode. The behavior varies depending on the disposability type of the resource.

Redefines the bucket intervals and reinitializes various data structures.

This function updates the bucket interval and reinitializes the intervals, buckets, fixed arcs, and fixed buckets. It also generates arcs and sorts them for each node.

◆ reset_fixed()

void BucketGraph::reset_fixed ( )
inline

Resets all fixed arcs in the graph.

This function iterates over each row in the fixed_arcs matrix and sets all elements to 0. It effectively clears any fixed arc constraints that may have been previously set.

void BucketGraph::reset_pool ( )
inline

Resets the forward and backward label pools.

This function resets both the forward (label_pool_fw) and backward (label_pool_bw) label pools to their initial states. It is typically used to clear any existing labels and prepare the pools for reuse.

◆ run_labeling_algorithms()

template<Stage state, Full fullness>
void BucketGraph::run_labeling_algorithms ( std::vector< double > & forward_cbar,
std::vector< double > & backward_cbar )

Runs forward and backward labeling algorithms in parallel and synchronizes the results.

T#his function creates tasks for forward and backward labeling algorithms using the provided scheduling mechanism. The tasks are executed in parallel, and the results are synchronized and stored in the provided vectors.

Definition at line 1118 of file BucketSolve.h.

1119 {
1120 // Create tasks for forward and backward labeling algorithms
1121
1122 auto forward_task =
1123 stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1124 return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, state, fullness>();
1125 });
1126
1127 auto backward_task =
1128 stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1129 return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Backward, state, fullness>();
1130 });
1131
1132 // Execute the tasks in parallel and synchronize
1133 auto work =
1134 stdexec::when_all(std::move(forward_task), std::move(backward_task)) |
1135 stdexec::then([&](auto forward_result, auto backward_result) {
1136 forward_cbar = std::move(forward_result);
1137 backward_cbar = std::move(backward_result);
1138 });
1139
1140 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));

◆ SCC_handler()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::SCC_handler ( )

Handles the computation of Strongly Connected Components (SCCs) for the BucketGraph.

This function processes the bucket graph to identify SCCs using Tarjan's algorithm. It extends the bucket graph with arcs defined by the Phi sets, computes the SCCs, and orders them topologically. It also sorts the buckets within each SCC based on their lower or upper bounds, depending on the direction. Finally, it splits the arcs for each SCC and removes duplicates.

Definition at line 725 of file BucketUtils.h.

725 {
726 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
727 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
728 auto &bucket_graph = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_graph, bw_bucket_graph);
729 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, std::vector<int>> extended_bucket_graph =
730 bucket_graph;
731
732 // Extend the bucket graph with arcs defined by the Phi sets
733 for (auto i = 0; i < extended_bucket_graph.size(); ++i) {
734 auto phi_set = Phi[i];
735 if (phi_set.empty()) continue;
736 for (auto &phi_bucket : phi_set) {
737 extended_bucket_graph[phi_bucket].push_back(i);
738 }
739 }
740
741 SCC scc_finder;
742 scc_finder.convertFromUnorderedMap(
743 extended_bucket_graph); // print extended bucket graph
744
745 auto sccs = scc_finder.tarjanSCC();
746 auto topological_order = scc_finder.topologicalOrderOfSCCs(sccs);
747
748#ifdef VERBOSE
749 // print SCCs and buckets in it
750 constexpr auto blue = "\033[34m";
751 constexpr auto reset = "\033[0m";
752 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
753 fmt::print("FW SCCs: \n");
754 } else {
755 fmt::print("BW SCCs: \n");
756 }
757 for (auto scc : topological_order) {
758 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
759 fmt::print("{}({}) -> {}", blue, scc, reset);
760 } else {
761 fmt::print("{}({}) -> {}", blue, scc, reset);
762 }
763 for (auto &bucket : sccs[scc]) {
764 fmt::print("{} ", bucket);
765 }
766 }
767 fmt::print("\n");
768#endif
769
770 std::vector<std::vector<int>> ordered_sccs;
771 ordered_sccs.reserve(sccs.size()); // Reserve space for all SCCs
772 for (int i : topological_order) {
773 ordered_sccs.push_back(sccs[i]);
774 }
775
776 auto sorted_sccs = sccs;
777 for (auto &scc : sorted_sccs) {
778 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
779 std::sort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) {
780 return buckets[a].lb[0] < buckets[b].lb[0];
781 });
782 } else {
783 std::sort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) {
784 return buckets[a].ub[0] > buckets[b].ub[0];
785 });
786 }
787 }
788
789 // iterate over nodes
790 for (auto &node : nodes) {
791 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
792 node.fw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
793 } else {
794 node.bw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
795 }
796 }
797
798 // Split arcs for each SCC
799 auto scc_ctr = 0;
800 for (const auto &scc : sccs) {
801 // Iterate over each bucket in the SCC
802 for (int bucket : scc) {
803 const int from_node_id =
804 buckets[bucket].node_id; // Cache source node ID
805 VRPNode &node = nodes[from_node_id]; // Cache node reference
806
807 // Define filtered arcs depending on the direction
808 auto &filtered_arcs = (D == Direction::Forward)
809 ? node.fw_arcs_scc[scc_ctr]
810 : node.bw_arcs_scc[scc_ctr];
811
812 // Reserve space for filtered arcs to avoid reallocations
813 filtered_arcs.reserve(
814 buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>().size());
815
816 // Create a map for faster arc lookups
817 // std::unordered_map<int, Arc> arc_map;
818 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, Arc> arc_map;
819 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
820 for (const auto &arc : node.fw_arcs) {
821 arc_map[arc.to] = arc;
822 }
823 } else {
824 for (const auto &arc : node.bw_arcs) {
825 arc_map[arc.to] = arc;
826 }
827 }
828
829 // Iterate over the arcs from the current bucket
830 for (const auto &arc :
831 buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>()) {
832 const int to_node_id =
833 buckets[arc.to_bucket]
834 .node_id; // Cache destination node ID
835
836 // Check if the arc exists in the map
837 auto it = arc_map.find(to_node_id);
838 if (it != arc_map.end()) {
839 // Add the arc to the filtered arcs
840 filtered_arcs.push_back(it->second);
841 }
842 }
843 }
844
845 // Increment SCC counter
846 ++scc_ctr;
847 }
848
849 for (auto &node : nodes) {
850 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
851 // Iterate over all SCCs for each node
852 for (auto &fw_arcs_scc : node.fw_arcs_scc) {
853 // Skip if the vector is empty
854 if (fw_arcs_scc.empty()) continue;
855
856 // Sort arcs by from_bucket and to_bucket
857 pdqsort(fw_arcs_scc.begin(), fw_arcs_scc.end(),
858 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
859 return std::tie(a.from, a.to) <
860 std::tie(b.from, b.to);
861 });
862
863 // Remove consecutive duplicates
864 auto last =
865 std::unique(fw_arcs_scc.begin(), fw_arcs_scc.end(),
866 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
867 return a.from == b.from && a.to == b.to;
868 });
869
870 // Erase the duplicates from the vector
871 fw_arcs_scc.erase(last, fw_arcs_scc.end());
872 }
873 } else {
874 // Iterate over all SCCs for each node
875 for (auto &bw_arcs_scc : node.bw_arcs_scc) {
876 // Skip if the vector is empty
877 if (bw_arcs_scc.empty()) continue;
878
879 // Sort arcs by from_bucket and to_bucket
880 pdqsort(bw_arcs_scc.begin(), bw_arcs_scc.end(),
881 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
882 return std::tie(a.from, a.to) <
883 std::tie(b.from, b.to);
884 });
885
886 // Remove consecutive duplicates
887 auto last =
888 std::unique(bw_arcs_scc.begin(), bw_arcs_scc.end(),
889 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
890 return a.from == b.from && a.to == b.to;
891 });
892
893 // Erase the duplicates from the vector
894 bw_arcs_scc.erase(last, bw_arcs_scc.end());
895 }
896 }
897 }
898
899 UnionFind uf(ordered_sccs);
900 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
901 fw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
902 fw_topological_order = topological_order;
903 fw_sccs = sccs;
904 fw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
905 fw_union_find = uf;
906 } else {
907 bw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
908 bw_topological_order = topological_order;
909 bw_sccs = sccs;
910 bw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
911 bw_union_find = uf;
912 }
913}
blue
constexpr const char * blue

◆ set_adjacency_list()

template<Symmetry SYM>
void BucketGraph::set_adjacency_list ( )

Definition at line 1126 of file BucketUtils.h.

1126 {
1127 // Clear existing arcs
1128 for (auto &node : nodes) {
1129 node.clear_arcs();
1130 }
1131
1132 // Compute clusters using MST-based clustering
1133 MST mst_solver(nodes,
1134 [this](int from, int to) { return this->getcij(from, to); });
1135
1136 // Compute theta dynamically based on the 90th percentile of edge weights
1137 auto mst = mst_solver.compute_mst();
1138 std::vector<double> edge_weights;
1139 edge_weights.reserve(mst.size());
1140 for (const auto &[weight, from, to] : mst) {
1141 edge_weights.push_back(weight);
1142 }
1143
1144 // Sort edge weights to compute the 90th percentile
1145 pdqsort(edge_weights.begin(), edge_weights.end());
1146 double theta = edge_weights[static_cast<size_t>(
1147 0.9 * edge_weights.size())]; // 75th percentile
1148 theta = theta / 100;
1149
1150 print_info("Computed theta: {}\n", theta);
1151
1152 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
1153 // print number of clusters
1154 print_info("Number of clusters: {}\n", clusters.size());
1155
1156 // Create job-to-cluster mapping
1157 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(nodes.size(), -1);
1158 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
1159 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) {
1160 job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id;
1161 }
1162 }
1163
1164 // Pre-calculate time resource index
1165 const int time_resource_idx =
1166 std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time") -
1167 options.resources.begin();
1168
1169 // Lambda for processing node arcs
1170 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &node, int from_bucket,
1171 std::vector<double> &res_inc) {
1172 using Arc = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>, double>;
1173 std::vector<Arc> forward_arcs;
1174 std::vector<Arc> reverse_arcs;
1175 forward_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
1176 reverse_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
1177
1178 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
1179 if (next_node.id == options.depot || node.id == next_node.id ||
1180 (options.pstep && (next_node.id == options.pstep_depot ||
1181 next_node.id == options.pstep_end_depot))) {
1182 continue;
1183 }
1184
1185 const auto travel_cost = getcij(node.id, next_node.id);
1186 const double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
1187 const int to_bucket = next_node.id;
1188
1189 if (from_bucket == to_bucket) continue;
1190
1191 // Calculate resource increments
1192 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
1193 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1194 res_inc[r] = r == time_resource_idx
1195 ? travel_cost + node.duration
1196 : node.consumption[r];
1197 }
1198 } else {
1199 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1200 res_inc[r] =
1201 r == time_resource_idx
1202 ? node.duration / 2 + travel_cost +
1203 next_node.duration / 2
1204 : (node.consumption[r] + next_node.consumption[r]) /
1205 2;
1206 }
1207 }
1208
1209 // Check resource feasibility
1210 bool feasible = true;
1211 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1212 if (node.lb[r] + res_inc[r] > next_node.ub[r]) {
1213 feasible = false;
1214 break;
1215 }
1216 }
1217 if (!feasible) continue;
1218
1219 // Calculate priorities
1220 const bool same_cluster =
1221 job_to_cluster[node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id];
1222 const double base_priority = same_cluster ? 5.0 : 1.0;
1223
1224 // Incorporate dual values (if available)
1225 double dual_value = -nodes[node.id].cost;
1226
1227 // Refine priority calculation
1228 const double priority =
1229 base_priority + 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time + dual_value;
1230 const double rev_priority = (same_cluster ? 1.0 : 5.0) +
1231 1.E-5 * node.start_time + dual_value;
1232
1233 forward_arcs.emplace_back(priority, next_node.id, res_inc,
1234 cost_inc);
1235 reverse_arcs.emplace_back(rev_priority, next_node.id, res_inc,
1236 cost_inc);
1237 }
1238
1239 // Sort arcs by priority (descending order) using pdqsort
1240 pdqsort(forward_arcs.begin(), forward_arcs.end(),
1241 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
1242 return std::get<0>(a) > std::get<0>(b);
1243 });
1244 pdqsort(reverse_arcs.begin(), reverse_arcs.end(),
1245 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
1246 return std::get<0>(a) > std::get<0>(b);
1247 });
1248
1249 // Add forward arcs
1250 for (const auto &[priority, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] :
1251 forward_arcs) {
1252 nodes[node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(
1253 node.id, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc, priority);
1254 }
1255
1256 // Add reverse arcs
1257 for (const auto &[priority, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] :
1258 reverse_arcs) {
1259 nodes[to_bucket].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(
1260 to_bucket, node.id, res_inc_local, cost_inc, priority);
1261 }
1262 };
1263
1264 // Process all nodes
1265 std::vector<double> res_inc(options.resources.size());
1266 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
1267 if (node.id != options.end_depot) {
1268 add_arcs_for_node(node, node.id, res_inc);
1269 }
1270 }
1271}

453 {
454 // Clear existing arcs for each node

457 }
458
459 // Step 1: Compute the clusters using MST-based clustering
460 MST mst_solver(nodes,
461 [&](int from, int to) { return this->getcij(from, to); });
462 double theta = 1.0; // Experiment with different values of θ
463 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
464
465 // Create a job-to-cluster mapping (cluster ID for each job/node)
466 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(
467 nodes.size(), -1); // Mapping from job (node) to cluster ID
468 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
469 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) {
470 job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id;
471 }
472 }
473
474 // Step 2: Modify add_arcs_for_node to give priority based on cluster
475 // membership
476 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &node, int from_bucket,
477 std::vector<double> &res_inc) {
478 using Arc = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>,
479 double>; // Arc: priority, to_node, resource
480 // increments, cost increment
481
482 std::vector<Arc> best_arcs;
483 best_arcs.reserve(nodes.size()); // Reserve space for forward arcs
484
485 std::vector<Arc> best_arcs_rev;
486 best_arcs_rev.reserve(nodes.size()); // Reserve space for reverse arcs
487
488 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
489 if (!manual_arcs.has_arc(node.id, next_node.id))
490 continue; // Skip arcs not in the manual list
491
492 if (next_node.id == options.depot || node.id == next_node.id)
493 continue; // Skip depot and same node
494
495 auto travel_cost =
496 getcij(node.id, next_node.id); // Calculate travel cost
497 double cost_inc =
498 travel_cost - next_node.cost; // Adjust cost increment by
499 // subtracting next node's cost
500
501 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
502 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
503 res_inc[r] = travel_cost +
504 node.duration; // Update resource increment
505 // based on node duration
506 } else {
507 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
508 }
509 }
510 // res_inc[TIME_INDEX] += travel_cost; // Add travel time to
511 // resource increment
512
513 int to_bucket = next_node.id;
514 if (from_bucket == to_bucket)
515 continue; // Skip arcs that loop back to the same bucket
516
517 bool feasible =
518 true; // Check feasibility based on resource constraints
519 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
520 if (node.lb[r] + res_inc[r] > next_node.ub[r]) {
521 feasible = false;
522 break;
523 }
524 }
525 if (!feasible) continue; // Skip infeasible arcs
526
527 // Step 3: Calculate priority based on cluster membership
528 double priority_value;
529 double reverse_priority_value;
530 if (job_to_cluster[node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id]) {
531 // Higher priority if both nodes are in the same cluster
532 priority_value =
533 5.0 +
534 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Adjust weight for
535 // same-cluster priority
536 reverse_priority_value =
537 1.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Adjust weight for
538 // same-cluster priority
539 } else {
540 // Lower priority for cross-cluster arcs
541 priority_value =
542 1.0 +
543 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Higher base value for
544 // cross-cluster arcs
545 reverse_priority_value =
546 5.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Higher base value for
547 // cross-cluster arcs
548 }
549
550 best_arcs.emplace_back(priority_value, next_node.id, res_inc,
551 cost_inc); // Store the forward arc
552 best_arcs_rev.emplace_back(reverse_priority_value, next_node.id,
553 res_inc,
554 cost_inc); // Store the reverse arc
555 }
556
557 // Add forward arcs from the current node to its neighbors
558 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs) {
559 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
560 nodes[node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(
561 node.id, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
562 priority_value); // Add forward arc
563 }
564
565 // Add reverse arcs from neighboring nodes to the current node
566 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs_rev) {
567 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
568 nodes[to_bucket].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(
569 to_bucket, node.id, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
570 priority_value); // Add reverse arc
571 }
572 };
573
574 // Step 4: Iterate over all nodes to set the adjacency list
575 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
576 if (VRPNode.id == options.end_depot)
577 continue; // Skip the last node (depot)
578
579 std::vector<double> res_inc(
580 intervals.size()); // Resource increment vector
581 add_arcs_for_node(VRPNode, VRPNode.id,
582 res_inc); // Add arcs for the current node
583 }
584}

Sets the distance matrix and calculates neighborhoods.

This function assigns the provided distance matrix to the internal distance matrix of the class and then calculates the neighborhoods based on the given number of nearest neighbors.

856 sPool.distance_matrix = distance_matrix;
857 sPool.setNodes(&nodes);
858 // sPool.setCutStorage(cut_storage);
859#endif
860
861 // Initialize the split
862 for (int i = 0; i < options.main_resources.size(); ++i) {
863 q_star.push_back((R_max[i] - R_min[i] + 1) / 2);

865}

◆ solve()

template<Symmetry SYM>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solve ( bool trigger = false)
inline

Solves the bucket graph optimization problem using a multi-stage bi-labeling algorithm.

The function iteratively adjusts the terminal time and handles different stages of the bi-labeling algorithm to find the optimal paths. The stages are adaptive and transition based on the inner objective values and iteration counts.

Definition at line 55 of file BucketSolve.h.

56 // Initialize the status as not optimal at the start
57 status = Status::NotOptimal;
58 if (trigger) {
59 transition = true;
60 fixed = false;
61 }
62
63 updateSplit(); // Update the split values for the bucket graph
64
65 // Placeholder for the final paths (labels) and inner objective value
66 std::vector<Label *> paths;
67
69 // ADAPTIVE STAGE HANDLING
71 // Stage 1: Apply a light heuristic (Stage::One)
72 if (s1 && depth == 0) {
73 stage = 1;
74 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::One>(); // Solve the problem with
75 // Stage 1 heuristic
76 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
77
78 // Transition from Stage 1 to Stage 2 if the objective improves or after
79 // 10 iterations
80 if (inner_obj >= -1 || iter >= 10) {
81 s1 = false;
82 s2 = true; // Move to Stage 2
83 }
84 }
85 // Stage 2: Apply a more expensive pricing heuristic (Stage::Two)
86 else if (s2 && depth == 0) {
87 s2 = true;
88 stage = 2;
89 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Two>(); // Solve the problem with
90 // Stage 2 heuristic
91 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
92
93 // Transition from Stage 2 to Stage 3 if the objective improves or after
94 // 800 iterations
95 if (inner_obj >= -100 || iter > 500) {
96 s2 = false;
97 s3 = true; // Move to Stage 3
98 }
99 }
100 // Stage 3: Apply a heuristic fixing approach (Stage::Three)
101 else if (s3 && depth == 0) {
102 stage = 3;
103 paths =
104 bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Three>(); // Solve the problem with
105 // Stage 3 heuristic
106 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
107
108 // Transition from Stage 3 to Stage 4 if the objective improves
109 // significantly
110 if (inner_obj >= -0.5) {
111 s4 = true;
112 s3 = false;
113 transition = true; // Prepare for transition to Stage 4
114 }
115 }
116 // Stage 4: Exact labeling algorithm with fixing enabled (Stage::Four)
117 else {
118 stage = 4;
119
120#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
121 // If transitioning to Stage 4, print a message and apply fixing
122 if (transition) {
123 // print_cut("Transitioning to stage 4\n");
124 bool original_fixed = fixed; // Store the original fixed status
125 fixed = true; // Enable fixing of buckets in Stage 4
126 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Four>(); // Solve the problem
127 // with Stage 4
128 transition = false; // End the transition period
129 fixed = original_fixed; // Restore the original fixed status
130 min_red_cost = paths[0]->cost; // Update the minimum reduced cost
131 iter++;
132 return paths; // Return the final paths
133 }
134#endif
135 // If not transitioning, continue with Stage 4 algorithm
136 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Four>();
137 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
138
139 auto rollback = false;
140 if (status != Status::Rollback) {
141 rollback = shallUpdateStep();
142 }
143 // auto rollback = false;
144 // auto rollback = false;
145 if (rollback) {
146 // s4 = false; // Rollback to Stage 3 if necessary
147 // s3 = true;
148 status = Status::Rollback; // Set status to rollback
149 return paths; // Return the paths after rollback
150 }
151 // If the objective improves sufficiently, set the status to separation
152 // or optimal
153 if (inner_obj > -1.0) {
154 ss = true; // Enter separation mode (for SRC handling)
155#if !defined(SRC) && !defined(SRC3)
156 status = Status::Optimal; // If SRC is not defined, set status to
157 // optimal
158 return paths; // Return the optimal paths
159#endif
160 status = Status::Separation; // If SRC is defined, set status to
161 // separation
162 }
163 }
164
165 iter++; // Increment the iteration counter
166
167 return paths; // Return the final paths after processing
168}

◆ solvePSTEP()

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solvePSTEP ( PSTEPDuals & inner_pstep_duals)

Solves the PSTEP problem and returns a vector of labels representing paths.

This function performs the following steps:

  1. Resets the pool.
  2. Initializes the mono algorithm.
  3. Runs a labeling algorithm in the forward direction.
  4. Iterates through the forward buckets and computes new labels.
  5. Filters and collects labels that meet the criteria.
Returns
std::vector<Label*> A vector of pointers to Label objects representing the paths.

Definition at line 24 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

24 {
25
26 const int CHUNK_SIZE = 10;
27
28 std::vector<Label *> all_paths;
29 std::vector<double> all_costs;
30 std::vector<int> all_firsts, all_lasts;
31
32 // Mutex for shared resources
33 std::mutex cuts_mutex;
34
35 const int JOBS = nodes.size();
36 exec::static_thread_pool pool(std::thread::hardware_concurrency());
37 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
38
39 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> tasks;
40 for (int i = 0; i < JOBS - 1; ++i) {
41 for (int j = 1; j < JOBS; ++j) {
42 if (i == j) continue;
43 tasks.emplace_back(i, j);
44 }
45 }
46 auto bulk_sender =
47 stdexec::bulk(stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + CHUNK_SIZE - 1) / CHUNK_SIZE, // Calculate total chunks
48 [&](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
49 // Calculate indices i, j from chunk_idx
50 size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * CHUNK_SIZE;
51 size_t end_idx = std::min(start_idx + CHUNK_SIZE, tasks.size());
52 for (size_t task_idx = start_idx; task_idx < end_idx; ++task_idx) {
53 auto [i, j] = tasks[task_idx];
54 BucketOptions sub_options;
55 auto sub_bg = BucketGraph(nodes, R_max[0], 20);
56 sub_bg.set_distance_matrix(distance_matrix);
57 sub_options.depot = i;
58 sub_options.end_depot = j;
59 // print i and j
60 sub_options.pstep = true; // print depot and end_depot
61 sub_options.max_path_size = options.max_path_size;
62 sub_options.min_path_size = options.min_path_size;
63 sub_bg.setOptions(sub_options);
64 sub_bg.setPSTEPduals(inner_pstep_duals);
65 sub_bg.setup();
66 sub_bg.reset_pool();
67 sub_bg.mono_initialization();
68 // Solve and get the paths
69 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(sub_bg.fw_buckets.size());
70 forward_cbar = sub_bg.labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, Stage::Four, Full::PSTEP>();
71 std::vector<Label *> paths;
72 // print paths.size
73 for (auto bucket : std::ranges::iota_view(0, sub_bg.fw_buckets_size)) {
74 auto bucket_labels = sub_bg.fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
75 // print bucket_labels size
76 for (auto label : bucket_labels) {
77 if (!label) continue;
78 auto new_label = compute_mono_label(label);
79 if ((new_label->nodes_covered.size() > 1) &&
80 (new_label->nodes_covered.back() == sub_options.end_depot) &&
81 (new_label->nodes_covered.size() >= options.min_path_size &&
82 new_label->nodes_covered.size() <= options.max_path_size)) {
83 paths.push_back(new_label);
84 }
85 }
86 }
87
88 // Check if no paths are found
89 if (paths.empty()) return;
90
91 // Lock to protect shared resources
92 {
93 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(cuts_mutex);
94 // Process the found paths
95 for (const auto &path : paths) { all_paths.push_back(path); }
96 }
97 // }
98 }
99 });
100
101 // Submit work to the thread pool and wait for completion
102 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
103 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
104
105 return all_paths;
106}

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solvePSTEP_by_MTZ ( )

Definition at line 356 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

356 {
357 int JOBS = nodes.size();
358
359 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> all_paths;
360 std::vector<double> all_costs;
361 std::vector<int> all_firsts;
362 std::vector<int> all_lasts;
363
364 auto duals = solveTSP(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, true);
365 auto max_pstep_iterations = 10;
366 for (auto z = 0; z < max_pstep_iterations; ++z) {
367 fmt::print("--------------------------------------\n");
368 fmt::print("Iteration {}\n", z);
369 auto three_two_duals = std::get<0>(duals);
370 auto three_three_duals = std::get<1>(duals);
371 auto three_five_duals = std::get<2>(duals);
372 PSTEPDuals inner_pstep_duals;
373
374 // Convert three_two to vector of pairs
375 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_two_tuples;
376 three_two_tuples.reserve(three_two_duals.size());
377 std::transform(three_two_duals.begin(), three_two_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_two_tuples),
378 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
379 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeTwoDualValues(std::move(three_two_tuples));
380
381 // Convert three_three to vector of pairs
382 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_three_tuples;
383 three_three_tuples.reserve(three_three_duals.size());
384 std::transform(three_three_duals.begin(), three_three_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_three_tuples),
385 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
386 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeThreeDualValues(std::move(three_three_tuples));
387
388 // Convert three_five to vector of pairs of pairs
389 std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, double>> arc_duals_tuples;
390 size_t total_size = 0;
391 for (const auto &row : three_five_duals) total_size += row.size();
392 arc_duals_tuples.reserve(total_size);
393
394 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
395 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
396 if (i != j) { // Skip diagonal or add specific condition if needed
397 arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]);
398 }
399 }
400 }
401 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
402
403 auto sub_paths = solvePSTEP(inner_pstep_duals);
404 // print sub_paths.size
405 auto paths_added = 0;
406 for (auto path : sub_paths) {
407 if (path->cost >= 0) { continue; }
408 paths_added++;
409 all_paths.push_back(path->getRoute());
410 all_costs.push_back(path->real_cost);
411 all_firsts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.front());
412 all_lasts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.back());
413 }
414 if (paths_added == 0) { break; }
415
416 duals = solveTSP(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix);
417 }
418 return {};
419}
BucketGraph::solveTSP
auto solveTSP(std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > &paths, std::vector< double > &path_costs, std::vector< int > &firsts, std::vector< int > &lasts, std::vector< std::vector< double > > &cost_matrix, bool first_time=false)

◆ solveTSP()

auto BucketGraph::solveTSP ( std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > & paths,
std::vector< double > & path_costs,
std::vector< int > & firsts,
std::vector< int > & lasts,
std::vector< std::vector< double > > & cost_matrix,
bool first_time = false )

Definition at line 228 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

230 {
231 auto n = cost_matrix.size();
232 MIPProblem model = MIPProblem("tsp", 0, 0);
233 if (first_time) { generateInitialPaths(n - 1, paths, path_costs, firsts, lasts, cost_matrix); }
234 // Define variables
235 int R = paths.size(); // Number of p-steps
236 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> x(R); // Binary variables for each p-step
237 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> u(n); // Continuous variables for each node
238
239 // Create binary variables x_r for each p-step r
240 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
241 // fmt::print("path_costs[{}] = {}\n", i, path_costs[i]);
242 std::string varname = "x_" + std::to_string(i);
243 double custo = path_costs[i];
244 x[i] = model.add_variable(varname, VarType::Continuous, 0.0, 1.0, custo);
245 }
246
247 // Create continuous variables u_i for each node i
248 for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
249 auto name = "u_" + std::to_string(i);
250 u[i] = model.add_variable(name, VarType::Continuous, 0.0, n - 1, 0.0);
251 }
252 model.setObjectiveSense(ObjectiveType::Minimize);
253
254 // Constraints (3.3) and (3.4):
255 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_two(n);
256 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_three(n);
257 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
258 LinearExpression in_constraint;
259 LinearExpression out_constraint;
260 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
261 if (firsts[r] == i) { in_constraint += x[r]; }
262 if (lasts[r] == i) { in_constraint -= x[r]; }
263 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end() && i != lasts[r]) {
264 out_constraint += x[r];
265 }
266 }
267 three_two[i] = model.add_constraint(in_constraint == 0, "33_" + std::to_string(i));
268 three_three[i] = model.add_constraint(out_constraint == 1, "34_" + std::to_string(i));
269 }
270
271 // Constraints (3.5): Avoid sub-tours using MTZ constraints
272 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_five_constraints;
273 std::vector<std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>> three_five_constraints_matrix(n, std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>(n));
274 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
275 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
276 if (i != j) {
277 LinearExpression r_sum;
278 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
279 for (int k = 0; k < paths[r].size() - 1; ++k) {
280 if (paths[r][k] == i && paths[r][k + 1] == j) { r_sum += x[r]; }
281 }
282 }
283 auto ctr = u[i] - u[j] + (n - 1) * r_sum <= (n - 2);
284 three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j] =
285 model.add_constraint(ctr, "mtz_" + std::to_string(i) + "_" + std::to_string(j));
286 }
287 }
288 }
289
290 for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
291 model.add_constraint(u[i] >= 0, "u_lb_" + std::to_string(i));
292 model.add_constraint(u[i] <= n - 1, "u_ub_" + std::to_string(i));
293 }
294
295#ifdef IPM
296 IPSolver *ipSolver = new IPSolver();
297 auto matrix = model.extractModelDataSparse();
298 ipSolver->run_optimization(matrix, 1e-2);
299
300 auto duals = ipSolver->getDuals();
301#else
302 auto duals = std::vector<double>(N_SIZE, 0.0);
303#endif
304 std::vector<double> three_two_duals(n, 0.0);
305 std::vector<double> three_three_duals(n, 0.0);
306 std::vector<std::vector<double>> three_five_duals(n, std::vector<double>(n + 1, 0.0));
307 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
308 three_two_duals[i] = options.three_two_sign * duals[three_two[i]->index()];
309 three_three_duals[i] = options.three_three_sign * duals[three_three[i]->index()];
310 }
311
312 // Extract duals for three_five constraints
313 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
314 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
315 if (i != j) {
316 three_five_duals[i][j] =
317 options.three_five_sign * (n - 1) * duals[three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j]->index()];
318 }
319 }
320 }
321#ifdef IPM
322 auto lp_obj = ipSolver->getObjective();
323#else
324 auto lp_obj = 0.0;
325#endif
326 fmt::print("LP Objective: {}\n", lp_obj);
327 GRBEnv &env = GurobiEnvSingleton::getInstance();
328 GRBModel *modelGRB = model.toGurobiModel(env).release(); // Release ownership from unique_ptr
329 // set model verbose
330 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_OutputFlag, 0);
331 // set model type as 2
332 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_Method, 2);
333
334 // convert x vars to int and solve again
335 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
336 modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).set(GRB_CharAttr_VType, GRB_BINARY);
337 }
338 modelGRB->update();
339 modelGRB->optimize();
340
341 if (modelGRB->get(GRB_IntAttr_Status) == GRB_OPTIMAL) {
342 fmt::print("Integer solution found with value: {}\n", modelGRB->get(GRB_DoubleAttr_ObjVal));
343 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
344 if (modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).get(GRB_DoubleAttr_X) > 0.5) {
345 fmt::print("x_{} = {}\n", i, modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).get(GRB_DoubleAttr_X));
346 // print path nodes
347 for (auto node : paths[i]) { fmt::print("{} ", node); }
348 fmt::print("\n");
349 }
350 }
351 }
352
353 return std::make_tuple(three_two_duals, three_three_duals, three_five_duals);
354}
generateInitialPaths
void generateInitialPaths(int n, std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > &paths, std::vector< double > &path_costs, std::vector< int > &firsts, std::vector< int > &lasts, const std::vector< std::vector< double > > &cost_matrix)

◆ solveTSPTW()

auto BucketGraph::solveTSPTW ( std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > & paths,
std::vector< double > & path_costs,
std::vector< int > & firsts,
std::vector< int > & lasts,
std::vector< std::vector< double > > & cost_matrix,
std::vector< double > & service_times,
std::vector< double > & time_windows_start,
std::vector< double > & time_windows_end,
bool first_time = false )

Definition at line 421 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

425 {
426 auto n = cost_matrix.size();
427 MIPProblem model = MIPProblem("tsptw", 0, 0);
428
429 if (first_time) {
430 generateInitialPathsTSPTW(n - 1, paths, path_costs, firsts, lasts, cost_matrix, time_windows_start,
431 time_windows_end, service_times);
432 }
433
434 // Variables
435 int R = paths.size();
436 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> x(R); // Binary variables for p-steps
437 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> omega(n); // Continuous variables for accumulated time
438
439 // Create binary variables x_r for each p-step r
440 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
441 x[i] = model.add_variable("x_" + std::to_string(i), VarType::Binary, 0.0, 1.0, path_costs[i]);
442 }
443
444 // Create continuous variables ω_i for time accumulation
445 for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
446 omega[i] = model.add_variable("omega_" + std::to_string(i), VarType::Continuous, 0.0, 1e8, 0.0);
447 }
448 model.setObjectiveSense(ObjectiveType::Minimize);
449
450 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_two(n);
451 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_three(n);
452 // Constraints (4.11): Flow balance for p-steps
453 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
454 LinearExpression flow_balance;
455 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
456 if (firsts[r] == i) flow_balance += x[r];
457 if (lasts[r] == i) flow_balance -= x[r];
458 }
459 three_two[i] = model.add_constraint(flow_balance == 0, "flow_" + std::to_string(i));
460 }
461
462 // Constraints (4.12): Visit each node at most once
463 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
464 LinearExpression visit_once;
465 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
466 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end()) { visit_once += x[r]; }
467 }
468 three_three[i] = model.add_constraint(visit_once <= 1, "visit_" + std::to_string(i));
469 }
470
471 // Constraint (4.13): Visit source exactly once
472 LinearExpression source_visit;
473 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
474 if (firsts[r] == 0) { source_visit += x[r]; }
475 }
476 model.add_constraint(source_visit == 1, "source");
477
478 // Constraints (4.14): Time accumulation with big-M
479 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_five_constraints;
480 std::vector<std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>> three_five_constraints_matrix(n, std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>(n));
481 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
482 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
483 if (i != j) {
484 double M_ij = 1e8;
485 LinearExpression time_acc;
486 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
487 for (size_t k = 0; k < paths[r].size() - 1; ++k) {
488 if (paths[r][k] == i && paths[r][k + 1] == j) { time_acc += x[r]; }
489 }
490 }
491 three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j] = model.add_constraint(
492 omega[j] - omega[i] - (cost_matrix[i][j] + service_times[i]) * time_acc - M_ij * time_acc >= -M_ij,
493 "time_" + std::to_string(i) + "_" + std::to_string(j));
494 }
495 }
496 }
497
498 // define omega[0] = 0
499
500 // Constraints (4.15): Time window constraints
501 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
502 LinearExpression node_used;
503 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
504 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end()) { node_used += x[r]; }
505 }
506 model.add_constraint(omega[i] - time_windows_start[i] * node_used >= 0, "tw_lb_" + std::to_string(i));
507 model.add_constraint(omega[i] - time_windows_end[i] * node_used <= 0, "tw_ub_" + std::to_string(i));
508 }
509
510// Solve the model
511#ifdef IPM
512 IPSolver *ipSolver = new IPSolver();
513 auto matrix = model.extractModelDataSparse();
514 ipSolver->run_optimization(matrix, 1e-2);
515
516 // Extract solution and duals
517 auto duals = ipSolver->getDuals();
518#else
519 auto duals = std::vector<double>(N_SIZE, 0.0);
520#endif
521 std::vector<double> three_two_duals(n, 0.0);
522 std::vector<double> three_three_duals(n, 0.0);
523 std::vector<std::vector<double>> three_five_duals(n, std::vector<double>(n + 1, 0.0));
524 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
525 three_two_duals[i] = options.three_two_sign * duals[three_two[i]->index()];
526 three_three_duals[i] = options.three_three_sign * duals[three_three[i]->index()];
527 fmt::print("Duals for node {}: 3.2 = {}, 3.3 = {}\n", i, three_two_duals[i], three_three_duals[i]);
528 }
529
530 // Extract duals for three_five constraints
531 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
532 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
533 if (i != j) {
534 three_five_duals[i][j] =
535 options.three_five_sign * (n - 1) * duals[three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j]->index()];
536 }
537 }
538 }
539
540 // Solution output and return
541 // fmt::print("LP Objective: {}\n", ipSolver->getObjective());
542
543 GRBEnv &env = GurobiEnvSingleton::getInstance();
544
545 GRBModel *modelGRB = model.toGurobiModel(env).release();
546 // set model verbose
547 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_OutputFlag, 1);
548 // set model type as 2
549 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_Method, 0);
550
551 modelGRB->update();
552 modelGRB->optimize();
553
554 return std::make_tuple(three_two_duals, three_three_duals, three_five_duals);
555}

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solveTSPTW_by_MTZ ( )

557 {
558 int JOBS = nodes.size();
559
560 // Initialize containers for storing paths, costs, firsts, and lasts
561 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> all_paths;
562 std::vector<double> all_costs;
563 std::vector<int> all_firsts;
564 std::vector<int> all_lasts;
565
566 // extract service time from nodes
567 std::vector<double> service_times;
568 std::vector<double> time_windows_start(JOBS, 0.0);
569 std::vector<double> time_windows_end(JOBS, 10000.0);
570 for (int i = 0; i < JOBS; ++i) {
571 time_windows_start[i] = nodes[i].lb[0];
572 time_windows_end[i] = nodes[i].ub[0];
573 service_times.push_back(nodes[i].duration);
574 }
575 // Step 1: Solve the initial TSPTW using the previously defined method
576 auto duals = solveTSPTW(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, service_times,
577 time_windows_start, time_windows_end, true);
578
579 // Step 2: Set up iteration for generating new paths using dual values
580 int max_pstep_iterations = 10;
581 for (int z = 0; z < max_pstep_iterations; ++z) {
582 fmt::print("--------------------------------------\n");
583 fmt::print("Iteration {}\n", z);
584
585 // Extract dual values for constraints
586 auto three_two_duals = std::get<0>(duals);
587 auto three_three_duals = std::get<1>(duals);
588 auto three_five_duals = std::get<2>(duals);
589 // auto arrival_time_duals = std::get<3>(duals);
590
591 PSTEPDuals inner_pstep_duals;
592
593 // Convert dual values for constraint (3.32)
594 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_two_tuples;
595 three_two_tuples.reserve(three_two_duals.size());
596 std::transform(three_two_duals.begin(), three_two_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_two_tuples),
597 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
598 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeTwoDualValues(std::move(three_two_tuples));
599
600 // Convert dual values for constraint (3.33)
601 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_three_tuples;
602 three_three_tuples.reserve(three_three_duals.size());
603 std::transform(three_three_duals.begin(), three_three_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_three_tuples),
604 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
605 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeThreeDualValues(std::move(three_three_tuples));
606
607 // Convert dual values for MTZ constraints (3.34) and time windows (3.35)
608 std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, double>> arc_duals_tuples;
609 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
610 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
611 if (i != j) { arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]); }
612 }
613 }
614 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
615
616 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
617 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
618 if (i != j) { // Skip diagonal or add specific condition if needed
619 arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]);
620 }
621 }
622 }
623 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
624
625 // Step 3: Solve the subproblem to generate new p-steps
626 fmt::print("Solving PSTEP subproblem\n");
627 auto sub_paths = solvePSTEP(inner_pstep_duals);
628 fmt::print("Subproblem solved\n");
629
630 // Add the new paths to the existing list if valid
631 int paths_added = 0;
632 for (auto path : sub_paths) {
633 if (path->cost >= 0) { continue; }
634 paths_added++;
635 all_paths.push_back(path->getRoute());
636 all_costs.push_back(path->real_cost);
637 all_firsts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.front());
638 all_lasts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.back());
639 }
640
641 // If no new paths were added, stop the iteration
642 if (paths_added == 0) { break; }
643
644 // Step 4: Re-solve the TSPTW with the updated set of p-steps
645 duals = solveTSPTW(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, service_times,
646 time_windows_start, time_windows_end);
647 }
648
649 return {};
650}

auto solveTSPTW(std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > &paths, std::vector< double > &path_costs, std::vector< int > &firsts, std::vector< int > &lasts, std::vector< std::vector< double > > &cost_matrix, std::vector< double > &service_times, std::vector< double > &time_windows_start, std::vector< double > &time_windows_end, bool first_time=false)

void BucketGraph::split_buckets ( )

623 {
624 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
625 auto &buckets_size =
626 assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
627 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
628 auto &num_buckets_index =
629 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
630
631 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
632 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
633 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
634
635 // Determine the base interval and other relevant values for each
636 // resource
637 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
638 total_ranges[r] = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
639 base_intervals[r] = total_ranges[r] / intervals[r].interval;
640 }
641
642 int original_num_buckets = buckets_size;
643
644 // To track splits per node_id
645 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, int> splits_per_node;
646
647 // Loop until the second-to-last bucket to avoid out-of-bounds access
648 for (int i = 0; i < original_num_buckets - 1; ++i) {
649 const auto &bucket = buckets[i];
650
651 // Calculate mid-point for each dimension
652 std::vector<double> mid_point(bucket.lb.size());
653 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
654 for (int r = 0; r < bucket.lb.size(); ++r) {
655 mid_point[r] = (bucket.lb[r] + base_intervals[r]) / 2.0;
656 }
657 } else {
658 for (int r = 0; r < bucket.lb.size(); ++r) {
659 mid_point[r] = (bucket.ub[r] - base_intervals[r]) / 2.0;
660 }
661 }
662
663 // Create two new buckets by splitting at the mid-point
664 Bucket bucket1, bucket2;
665 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
666 bucket1 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, bucket.ub);
667 bucket2 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, mid_point, bucket.ub);
668 } else {
669 bucket1 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, bucket.ub);
670 bucket2 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, mid_point);
671 }
672
673 // Manually shift elements to the right to make space for the new
674 // bucket
675 for (int j = original_num_buckets; j > i + 1; --j) {
676 buckets[j] = buckets[j - 1];
677 }
678
679 // Insert the new bucket at position i+1
680 buckets[i + 1] = bucket2;
681
682 // Replace the current bucket with the first half (bucket1)
683 buckets[i] = bucket1;
684
685 // Track the number of splits for this node_id
686 splits_per_node[bucket.node_id]++;
687
688 // Since we added a new bucket at i+1, increment i to skip over the
689 // new bucket
690 ++i;
691 ++original_num_buckets; // Adjust the count to account for the new
692 // bucket
693 }
694
695 // Update the global bucket size
696 buckets_size = original_num_buckets;
697
698 // Now update the num_buckets_index for each node_id based on the final
699 // positions
700 int current_index = 0;
701 for (int i = 0; i < original_num_buckets; ++i) {
702 const auto &bucket = buckets[i];
703 if (num_buckets_index[bucket.node_id] == -1) {
704 num_buckets_index[bucket.node_id] = current_index;
705 }
706 current_index++;
707 }
708 }

◆ UpdateBucketsSet()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::UpdateBucketsSet ( double theta,
const Label * label,
ankerl::unordered_dense::set< int > & Bbidi,
int & current_bucket,
std::vector< uint64_t > & Bvisited )

Updates the set of buckets based on the given parameters.

This function inserts the current bucket into the visited set and then performs various checks and updates based on the provided theta, label, Bbidi, current_bucket, and Bvisited parameters.

Definition at line 41 of file BucketJump.h.

44 {
45 // Precompute values and assign references for the direction-specific data
46
47 auto &Phi_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_bw, Phi_fw);
48 auto &buckets_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(bw_buckets, fw_buckets);
49 auto &c_bar_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(bw_c_bar, fw_c_bar);
50
51 const int bucketLnode = label->node_id;
52 std::vector<int> bucket_stack;
53 bucket_stack.reserve(10); // Pre-allocate space for the stack
54 bucket_stack.push_back(current_bucket);
55
56 // Lambda to compare bitmaps
57 auto bitmaps_conflict = [&](const Label *L1, const Label *L2) {
58 for (size_t i = 0; i < L1->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
59 if ((L1->visited_bitmap[i] & L2->visited_bitmap[i]) != 0) {
60 return true;
61 }
62 }
63 return false;
64 };
65
66 while (!bucket_stack.empty()) {
67 int curr_bucket = bucket_stack.back();
68 bucket_stack.pop_back();
69 const size_t segment =
70 curr_bucket >> 6; // Determine the segment for the current bucket
71 const size_t bit_position =
72 curr_bucket & 63; // Determine the bit position within the segment
73 Bvisited[segment] |=
74 (1ULL << bit_position); // Set the bit corresponding to the current
75 // bucket as visited
76 // print current bucket
77 const int bucketLprimenode = buckets_opposite[curr_bucket].node_id;
78 double cost = getcij(bucketLnode, bucketLprimenode);
79
80 RCC_MODE_BLOCK(auto arc_dual =
81 arc_duals.getDual(bucketLnode, bucketLprimenode);
82 cost -= arc_dual;)
83 // Stop exploring if the cost exceeds the threshold
84 if (label->cost + cost + c_bar_opposite[curr_bucket] >= theta) {
85 continue;
86 }
87
88 // Only process if bucket is not already in Bbidi
89 if (Bbidi.insert(curr_bucket).second) {
90 const auto opposite_labels =
91 buckets_opposite[curr_bucket].get_labels();
92 for (const auto &L : opposite_labels) {
93 if (label->node_id == L->node_id ||
94 bitmaps_conflict(label, L)) {
95 continue; // Skip labels with the same node ID or
96 // conflicting bitmaps
97 }
98
99 const VRPNode &L_last_node = (D == Direction::Forward)
100 ? nodes[label->node_id]
101 : nodes[L->node_id];
102
103 bool violated = false;
104 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
105 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
106 const double time_constraint =
107 (D == Direction::Forward)
108 ? label->resources[TIME_INDEX] + cost +
109 L_last_node.duration
110 : L->resources[TIME_INDEX] + cost +
111 L_last_node.duration;
112
113 if (time_constraint >
114 ((D == Direction::Forward)
115 ? L->resources[TIME_INDEX]
116 : label->resources[TIME_INDEX])) {
117 violated = true;
118 break;
119 }
120 } else {
121 const double resource_constraint =
122 (D == Direction::Forward)
123 ? label->resources[r] +
124 L_last_node.consumption[r]
125 : L->resources[r] + L_last_node.consumption[r];
126
127 if (resource_constraint > ((D == Direction::Forward)
128 ? L->resources[r]
129 : label->resources[r])) {
130 violated = true;
131 break;
132 }
133 }
134 }
135
136 if (violated) {
137 continue;
138 }
139
140 // Insert the current bucket into Bbidi if the total cost is
141 // below theta
142 if (label->cost + cost + L->cost < theta) {
143 Bbidi.emplace(curr_bucket);
144 }
145 }
146 }
147
148 // Add neighboring buckets to the stack, only if not visited
149 for (int b_prime : Phi_opposite[curr_bucket]) {
150 const size_t segment_prime =
151 b_prime >>
152 6; // Determine the segment for the neighboring bucket
153 const size_t bit_position_prime =
154 b_prime & 63; // Determine the bit position within the segment
155
156 // If the neighboring bucket hasn't been visited, push it onto the
157 // stack
158 if ((Bvisited[segment_prime] & (1ULL << bit_position_prime)) == 0) {
159 bucket_stack.push_back(b_prime);
160 }
161 }
162 }
163}

◆ updateSplit()

void BucketGraph::updateSplit ( )
inline

Definition at line 820 of file BucketGraph.h.

820 {
821 const double BASE_IMBALANCE_THRESHOLD =
822 0.15; // Slightly lower base threshold
823 const double MIN_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR = 0.02;
824 const double MAX_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR = 0.10;
825 const double LEARNING_RATE_DECAY =
826 0.99; // Decay factor for learning rate
827 const double EMA_ALPHA = 0.1; // Smoothing factor for EMA
828
829 static double learning_rate =
830 MAX_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR; // Initialize learning rate
831 static double ema_imbalance_ratio =
832 0.0; // Initialize EMA of imbalance ratio
833
834 // Calculate label counts and ratios
835 double fw_labels = static_cast<double>(n_fw_labels);
836 double bw_labels = static_cast<double>(n_bw_labels);
837 double total_labels = fw_labels + bw_labels;
838
839 // Skip if we don't have enough labels for meaningful adjustment
840 if (total_labels < 10) return;
841
842 // Calculate imbalance ratio
843 double fw_ratio = fw_labels / total_labels;
844 double bw_ratio = bw_labels / total_labels;
845 double imbalance_ratio = std::abs(fw_ratio - bw_ratio);
846
847 // Update EMA of imbalance ratio
848 ema_imbalance_ratio =
849 EMA_ALPHA * imbalance_ratio + (1 - EMA_ALPHA) * ema_imbalance_ratio;
850
851 // Dynamic threshold based on total number of labels
852 // As we get more labels, we can be more sensitive to imbalance
853 double dynamic_threshold =
854 BASE_IMBALANCE_THRESHOLD * std::exp(-total_labels / 1000.0);
855
856 if (ema_imbalance_ratio > dynamic_threshold) {
857 // Calculate adaptive adjustment factor
858 // More severe imbalance = larger adjustment
859 double severity = (ema_imbalance_ratio - dynamic_threshold) /
860 (1.0 - dynamic_threshold);
861 double adjustment_factor =
862 MIN_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR +
863 (MAX_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR - MIN_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR) * severity;
864
865 // Adjust learning rate based on the severity of imbalance
866 learning_rate *= LEARNING_RATE_DECAY;
867
868 // Adjust based on resource range
869 double resource_range = R_max[options.main_resources[0]] -
870 R_min[options.main_resources[0]];
871 double base_adjustment =
872 adjustment_factor * resource_range * learning_rate;
873
874 // Scale adjustment based on current split position
875 for (size_t i = 0; i < q_star.size(); ++i) {
876 double relative_pos =
877 (q_star[i] - R_min[options.main_resources[0]]) /
878 resource_range;
879
880 // Smaller adjustments near boundaries
881 double position_factor =
882 4.0 * relative_pos * (1.0 - relative_pos);
883
884 // Determine direction and apply adjustment
885 double direction = (bw_labels > fw_labels) ? 1.0 : -1.0;
886 double final_adjustment =
887 base_adjustment * position_factor * direction;
888
889 // Apply adjustment with boundary protection
890 q_star[i] = std::clamp(q_star[i] + final_adjustment,
891 R_min[options.main_resources[0]] +
892 resource_range * 0.1, // Lower bound
893 R_max[options.main_resources[0]] -
894 resource_range * 0.1 // Upper bound
895 );
896 }
897
898 if (std::abs(base_adjustment) > resource_range * 0.05) {
899 print_info(
900 "Split adjustment: imbalance={:.3f}, adjustment={:.3f}, "
901 "learning_rate={:.3f}\n",
902 ema_imbalance_ratio, base_adjustment, learning_rate);
903 }
904 }
905 }
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ updateStepSize()

bool BucketGraph::updateStepSize ( )
inline

Definition at line 909 of file BucketGraph.h.

909 {
910 bool updated = false;
911 if (bucket_interval >= 100) {
912 return false;
913 }
914 // compute the mean of dominance_checks_per_bucket
915 double mean_dominance_checks = 0.0;
916 for (size_t i = 0; i < dominance_checks_per_bucket.size(); ++i) {
917 mean_dominance_checks += dominance_checks_per_bucket[i];
918 }
919 auto step_calc =
920 mean_dominance_checks / non_dominated_labels_per_bucket;
921 if (step_calc > 100) {
922 // redefine_counter = 0;
923 print_info("Increasing bucket interval to {}\n",
924 bucket_interval + 25);
925 bucket_interval = bucket_interval + 25;
926 redefine(bucket_interval);
927 updated = true;
928 fixed = false;
929 }
930 return updated;
931 }
